channel.title: Mom's Cookbook on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Mom's Cookbook on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: “bush Bread” (bannock) for 1 Scone
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ cup margarine

Instructions

Mix all the above ingredients together to form a cornmeal-like mixture.

Put this mixture in plastic bag for transporting over portages.

When needed, mix with water in a bowl, then dump it in frying pan and cook till brown. (Not too fast.)

item.title: “dirt” Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg (4-serving size) Jello Chocolate
  • 1 cup cold milk
  • instant pudding
  • 1 1-L Cool Whip, thawed
  • 20 chocolate sandwich cookies
  • 1-1/2 cups miniature marshmallows
  • chopped
  • 1 graham crumb crust
  • Gummy worms for decoration

Instructions

Prepare pudding according to package directions reducing milk to 1 cup. Fold in whipped topping. Stir in 2/3 of the cookies and all the marshmallows. Spoon into crust. Sprinkle top with remaining cookies. Decorate with gummy worms. Freeze until firm – about 4 hours. Remove from freezer 10 minutes before serving for easy slicing. Makes 6 to 8 servings. For best results, use only Jello instant pudding and Cool Whip topping.

item.title: Almond Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Line cookie sheet (ll x l7) with foil. Grease foil.
  • Put graham wafers (single layer) on foil. Top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds.
  • Boil ¾ cup brown sugar with l cup butter for 4 or 5 minutes.
  • Spoon this mixture over graham wafers and almonds
  • Bake l0 minutes at 350 degrees. Cut them in half while still hot.
  • Graham wafers
  • l/2 pkg flaked almonds
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • l cup butter

item.title: Almond Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Line cookie sheet (ll x l7) with foil. Grease foil.
  • Put graham wafers (single layer) on foil. Top with l/2 pkg of flaked almonds.
  • Boil ¾ cup brown sugar with l cup butter for 4 or 5 minutes.
  • Spoon this mixture over graham wafers and almonds
  • Bake l0 minutes at 350 degrees. Cut them in half while still hot.
  • Graham wafers
  • l/2 pkg flaked almonds
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • l cup butter

item.title: Ann’s Indian Fry Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 heaping Tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp table salt
  • water to mix and oil or lard to fry in

Instructions

METHOD: In a large bowl combine all dry ingredients. Then slowly add cold water as needed. The texture is a thick texture, thicker than pancake batter. Mix well.

Flour the area where you will be rolling out the dough. Drop the whole dough chunk onto flour, and freely sprinkle more flour on this dough. Begin to knead as though making bread, making sure dough is not sticking to your hands. If that happens, just ad a little more flour.

item.title: Apple Pie – (purity Cookbook)
item.description:
Instructions

Preheat over to 375 degrees.

For each pie, I combine slightly more than ½ cup of sugar (more if apples are tart) with ½ tsp cinnamon and a dash of salt.

Prepare pastry and line a 9” pie plate with the rolled pastry..Roll out the top pastry.

Peel and core apples. (I usually used 6 medium-sized apples). I half fill the pastry-lined pie plate with thinly sliced apples. Sprinkle on half of the sugar mixture. Finish filling the pie plate with thinly sliced apples, heaping them in the center. Sprinkle the rest of the sugar mixture on top. Dot with 1 Tablespoon of butter.

item.title: Asiago Drop Biscuits – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 2 Tbsp sugar (can use Splenda)
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/3 cup margarine
  • 1/3 cup grated Asiago cheese
  • 2 Tbsp chopped herbs (rosemary, thyme, sage,
  • oregano – these can be fresh or dried herbs)

Instructions

Combine dry ingredients. Cut in margarine. Stir in cheese and herbs. Pour buttermilk over flour mixture and stir only enough to form rough batter (don’t over mix)

item.title: Aunt Gert’s Hamburg Soup – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp butter
  • 3 small onions, chopped
  • 3 large carrots, sliced
  • 3 celery stalks, diced
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 – 16 oz can diced tomatoes
  • 3 medium potatoes, diced
  • 1/3 cup macaroni
  • 6 cups water
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Melt butter, add ground beef and cook slightly. Add onions, tomatoes, salt, pepper and water. Bring to a boil. Cover and simmer for 1 hour. Add vegetables and simmer 1 hour longer. Stir in macaroni during the last 15 minutes.

item.title: Baked Indian Pudding – Aboriginal Cooking
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 cups milk
  • 2 level Tablespoon butter
  • 6 eggs
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp ginger
  • 1 cup Indian meal (Cornmeal)
  • 1 cup powdered sugar (Fruit)

Instructions

Scald 4 cups milk. Mix Indian Meal with 1 cup cold milk and stir into hot milk. Add butter. Continue to stir until mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat.

Beat eggs until fluffy with the sugar and add nutmeg and ginger, then stir the whole mixture together. Pour into a buttered casserole or deep 12 x 8” baking dish.

item.title: Bannock
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups flour (730 ml)
  • 3 level Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 Tbsp sugar
  • 1 cup cold water
  • 1 Tbsp (heaping) lard
  • ¾ tsp salt

Instructions

Mix together the flour, baking powder, salt and sugar in large bowl. Add the lard, cut into small pieces. Mix and make a well in the middle. Add water and mix again. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour. Pat and form into a cake. Deep fry in enough lard or oil so the mixture floats OR bake over coals on a stick like hot dogs. Cook about 20 minutes. Tastes great alone or buttered, with molasses, honey or corn syrup.

item.title: Barbecue Marinade – for Meat or Poultry
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup prepared barbecue sauce
  • ¼ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ cup Realemon lemon juice
  • 1 Tbsp packed brown sugar

Instructions

In small bow, combine barbecue sauce, oil, lemon juice and sugar. Pour over meat or poultry. Cover and refrigerate six hours or overnight, turning occasionally. Remove meat from marine; grill or broil as desired, basting frequently with marinade. Makes about 1 cup.

BEER MARINADE – For beef or pork

item.title: Barbecue Sauce
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup Canola oil
  • ½ cup chili sauce
  • 1 cup vinegar
  • 1 Tbsp chopped onion
  • 1 clove garlic
  • 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • ½ tsp chili powder (optional)
  • ¼ cup brown sugar

Instructions

Measure all ingredients into bowl. Mix until thoroughly blended.

YIELD: 2 cups

NOTE: Use Sauce for Barbecued Frankfurters, Chicken, Fish, Pork Chops or Ribs.

item.title: Beet Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Boil, peel and dice beets to make 16 cups.
  • ½ cup flour
  • 1 Tbsp dry mustard
  • 2-1/2 cups white vinegar
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 Tbsp salt

Instructions

Mix until well blended and bring to a boil. Boil until sauce is thickened. Add diced beets. Pour hot mixture into hot sealer jars. Seal and let cool. Vacuum seal is accomplished when jar lids “pop” inward.

item.title: Betty Lance’s Award-winning Banic
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 to 3 cups flour
  • Pinch of salt
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp baking powder
  • 2 Tbsp margarine – melted
  • 1-1/2 cups milk (enough milk to moisten – like dumplings)

Instructions

Bake at 350 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes until golden brown.

Use a cast iron frying pan or an 8 x 8 cake pan.

item.title: Betty’s Caesar Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup salad oil
  • ¼ cup lemon juice
  • 1 egg
  • 2 garlic cloves
  • Salt and pepper

Instructions

Place these ingredients in a blender and blend thoroughly. Chill.

Iceberg lettuce or Romaine lettuce Croutons Bacon, cooked crispy and crumbled

Tear lettuce in bite-sized pieces into salad bowl. Add dressing, bacon bits and Caesar salad mix to taste. Top with croutons.

item.title: Beverly’s Spinach Quiche
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 box chopped spinach or broccoli
  • l/2 chopped onion
  • l/2 cup bell pepper
  • l cup cheese
  • ¾ cup Bisquick
  • 3 eggs
  • l/2 tsp salt
  • l/4 tsp pepper

Instructions

Spray Pam in pan. Put first 4 ingredients (spinach, onion, bell pepper, cheese) in bottom of pie plate. Mix rest with mixer to pour over other ingredients. Bake at 400 degrees for 35 minutes.

item.title: Blanc Mange
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup sugar
  • 3 Tbsp cornstarch
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2-1/4 cup milk

Instructions

Mix sugar, starch and salt in top of double boiler. Add milk, mix and cook until thickened.

Yum, Yum!

item.title: Blueberry Soup – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups blueberries
  • 1 cup water
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 3 Tbsp lemon juice
  • Pinch of cinnamon
  • Pinch of cloves
  • ½ tsp coriander
  • 1 cup yogurt or cream

Instructions

In a blender, blend blueberries until pureed. Place in cooking pot and add remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer 5 minutes (covered). Remove from heat and cool. Once cooled, add one cup of yogurt or one cup of cream.

item.title: Bran Bannock
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 cup bran
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp soda
  • ½ tsp cream of tartar
  • 1 Tbsp melted shortening
  • 1 cup sour milk

Instructions

Mix dry ingredients, add the melted shortening, then sour milk. Pour into a greased baking pan and bake in a moderate oven for about 40 minutes.

item.title: Bread & Butter Pickles (colette Tremblay)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 24 cukes, sliced, let stand in salt 2 hrs.
  • Rinse in cold water, drain.
  • 12 onions, sliced
  • 4 cups vinegar
  • 2-1/2 cups white sugar
  • 1 tsp ginger
  • 1 tsp turmeric
  • 1 tsp mustard seed
  • 1 tsp celery seed

Instructions

Put cukes and onions in vinegar solution and just bring to a boil. Take off heat immediately and pack cukes & onions in jars.

Bring solution to a boil again and pour into jars. Seal

item.title: Bread Pudding
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups bread crumbs
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp cinnamon or nutmeg
  • ½ cup seeded raisins
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups milk
  • ¼ cup of butter

Instructions

Scald milk and add butter. Beat eggs slightly and add to milk with sugar, salt, spice and raisins. Pour over bread crumbs and mix well. Pour into a greased deep casserole and bake in a 350 degree oven for 45 minutes. Serve with cream or lemon sauce.

item.title: Broccoli Salad – (bill & Heather’s Cookbook)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup raisins
  • ½ cup shelled sunflower seeds
  • 1 medium red onion, chopped
  • ½ lb crisp, crumbled bacon
  • 3 heads broccoli florets

Instructions

Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.

Dressing: ½ cup Miracle Whip 2 tsp rice vinegar 1/3 cup white sugar (or Splenda)

Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.

item.title: Bucket Pickles
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups granulated sugar
  • 2 cups white vinegar
  • 2 Tbsp coarse pickling salt
  • 1 tsp turmeric
  • 1 tsp celery seed
  • 1 tsp mustard seed
  • 1 lg red pepper, seeded and cut in slices
  • 1 lg green pepper, seeded and cut in slices
  • 1 lg Spanish onion, cut in slices
  • 5 pickling cucumbers with peel, thinly sliced

Instructions

Measure the first 6 ingredients in a 4 quart ice cream bucket. Stir.

item.title: Butter Mallow Squaraes
item.description:
Instructions

BASE: 1/3 cup butter 1 cup lightly packed brown sugar 1 egg 1 tsp vanilla ¾ cup all-purpose flour 1 tsp baking powder ¼ tsp salt ½ cup chopped pecans (I used walnuts) FILLING: 2-1/2 cups miniature marshmallows TOPPING: ¼ cup butter ½ cup lightly packed brown sugar 2 Tbsp milk 1 cup sifted icing sugar 32 pecan halves (optional) BASE: Melt butter in large saucepan. Stir in brown sugar, egg and vanilla. Blend together flour, baking powder and salt. Stir dry ingredients into butter mixture along with pecans or walnuts. Spread batter in greased 8” square pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 – 30 minutes. Immediately scatter KRAFT marshmallows over entire surface of hot cake. Press gently to flatten. TOPPING: Melt butter in saucepan. Add brown sugar and simmer over low heat, stirring constantly for 2 minutes. Add milk, stirring until mixture just return to boil. Remove from heat. Gradually add icing sugar, beating with a wooden spoon after each addition until smooth. If necessary, thin icing with an additional few drops of milk. Spread quickly over top of marshmallows. Decorate with pecans, if desired. Cool before cutting into squares. Makes 20 squares. NOTE: I found squares are easier to cut and remove from pan if they are still a bit warm when I cut them.

item.title: Butter Tart Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Base:
  • 1-1/4 cup flour
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup butter
  • Filling:
  • 1/3 cup butter
  • 1 egg
  • 2 tbsp cream
  • 1/2 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1/2 cup raisins (or walnuts)
  • 1 cup brown sugar

Instructions

Grease pan lightly, especially around the edges to make it easier to remove edges pieces. Mix base ingredients together and press firmly in a 9-inch square pan. Mix filling together. Pour over base and bake at 350 degrees until done. (25 to 30 minutes)

item.title: Butter Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup soft butter
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp milk
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • raisins or walnuts (optional)

Instructions

Cream butter and brown sugar. Add milk, vanilla and egg, and beat well, preferably with an electric beater. Half fill pastry-lined tart tins and bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.

item.title: Butterscotch Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 tbsp butter
  • 1-1/2 cups of milk
  • 1-1/2 cups brown sugar
  • 6 tbsp flour
  • ¾ cup boiling water
  • pinch of salt
  • 4 egg yolks

Instructions

Melt butter, add sugar and allow to brown. Gradually add boiling water. In medium size bowl, beat egg yolks; add a small amount of the measured milk and the flour. Beat until smooth. Then add remainder of milk and salt. Pour this slowly into sugar mixture and cook until thick, stirring constantly.

item.title: Butterscotch Pie (carmen Zimmerman)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 9”baked pie shell
  • 2-1/2 cups milk
  • 1 cup dark brown sugar
  • 3 egg yolks, slightly beaten
  • 3-1/2 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 3 tbsp butter

Instructions

Combine sugar, cornstarch and salt in top of a double boiler. Stir in cold milk. Cook over boiling water until thickened – stirring constantly. Cover and cook 15 minutes longer.

Stir a little of the hot mixture into slightly beaten egg yolks. Add to remaining mixture in double boiler and cook for 2 minutes over hot, not boiling, water, stirring constantly. Add the butter and vanilla and let cool. Pour into the baked pie shell.

item.title: Caesar Salad Dressing – No. 2
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 egg yolks
  • ½ clove garlic
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 2/3 cup olive oil

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in a blender. Mix well. Serve with romaine lettuce, croutons and bacon. Makes 1-1/2 cup dressing.

item.title: Caesar Salad Seasoning Mix
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp ground pepper
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • 1 cup (1/4 lb) grated Romano
  • ½ tsp crushed oregano
  • or Parmesan cheese or a blend
  • ½ tsp M.S.G. (optional)
  • or Parmesan and Romano

Instructions

Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Store in a covered jar at room temperature. Use as the seasoning in Caesar Salad. It may also be sprinkled over other tossed salads and used as a topping for casseroles and creamed soups.

item.title: Caesar Scalloped Potatoes
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Preparation: 10 minutes
  • Cook: 22 minutes
  • 2 Tbsp margarine or butter
  • 1 Tbsp all-purpose Flour
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1 pouch Loney’s Onion Soup mix
  • ½ cup parmesan cheese, grated
  • 1 tsp Dijon mustard
  • ½ tsp black pepper
  • Pinch garlic powder
  • 4 cups peeled potatoes, sliced

Instructions

In large, deep microwaveable casserole, melt margarine or butter at high. Blend in flour, microwave at High 30 seconds. Whisk in milk and Soup Mix; microwave at High 4 to 6 Minutes, stirring twice, until mixture thickens slightly. Stir in cheese, mustard, pepper, garlic powder and potatoes. Microwave at High, covered, 12 to 16 minutes or until potatoes are tender. Stir twice during cooking.

item.title: Cajun Spice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tbsp sweet paprika
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp onion powder
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tsp cayenne pepper
  • ½ tsp white pepper
  • 1 tsp black pepper
  • ½ tsp dried thyme
  • ½ tsp dried oregano

Instructions

Spread melted butter on cold fish that has been wiped dry. Sprinkle spices on fish. Cook fish in ungreased very hot iron frying pan. NOTE: Fish should be sliced fairly thin so that it will cook right through without burning on the outside. Fish done this way cooks very rapidly.

item.title: Campbell’s Broth for Savoury Rice
item.description:
Instructions

EASY HERBED RICE – Just substitute Campbell’s Chicken Broth for the water when cooking rice. During the simmering time, add your favourite fresh or dried herbs, such as oregano, thyme or parsley.

EASY MUSHROOM RICE – Cook your rice with Campbell’s Chicken or Beef Broth instead of water. Stir fry fresh sliced mushrooms in a saucepan. Add to simmering rice at the end of cook time.

EASY SAVOURY RICE PILAF – Simply cook rice in Campbell’s Chicken or Beef Broth, instead of water. During the last 10 minutes of the simmering time, add fresh or frozen diced vegetables.

item.title: Caramel Dumplings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter or margarine
  • 1-1/2 cups packed brown sugar
  • 1-1/2 cups water

Instructions

DUMPLINGS; 1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour ½ cup sugar 2 tsp baking powder ½ tsp salt ½ cup milk 2 tbsp butter or margarine, softened 2 tsp vanilla extract ½ cup coarsely chopped peeled apple (optional)

In a skillet, heat the unsoftened butter, brown sugar and water to boiling. Reduce heat to simmer. Meanwhile, mix together all dumpling ingredients. Drop by tablespoonfuls into the simmering sauce. Cover tightly and simmer 20 minutes. Do not lift lid. Serve warm with cream or ice cream, if desired.

item.title: Carnation “can’t Fail” 5-minute Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup (Small can) undiluted Carnation evaporated milk
  • 1-2/3 cup sugar
  • 1-1/2 cup (6 medium) diced marshmallows
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1-1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup chopped nuts

Instructions

Mix Carnation, sugar and salt together in sauce pan over low heat. Heat to boiling; cook 5 minute, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Add marshmallows, chocolate chips, vanilla and nuts. Stir 1 to 2 minutes or until marshmallows melt. Pour into buttered pan (9” square). Cool. Then cut into squares. Indeed it is wonderful to know the secret of making fudge that is smooth and creamy – perfect every time.

item.title: Carnation Five-minute Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-2/3 cup granulated sugar
  • 2/3 cup evaporated milk
  • 2 tbsp butter
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 cups miniature marshmallows
  • 1-1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

In medium saucepan, combine sugar, evaporated milk, butter and salt. Stir over medium heat until mixture comes to a full boil. Boil 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat Stir in marshmallows, chocolate chips, nuts and vanilla. Stir vigorously until marshmallows melt and blend. Pour into foil-lined 8” square pan. Chill until set. Cut into 1-inch squares to serve. Store in refrigerator in sealed container. Makes 64 squares. Variations: Omit chopped walnuts and substitute one of the following: 1 cup chopped toasted hazelnuts 1 cup chopped toasted almonds, plus 1 tsp almond extract 1 cup chopped well-drained maraschino cherries plus ½ cup chopped nuts ¾ cup finely chopped candied ginger

item.title: Carnival Chip Dainties
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup melted butter
  • 1 6-oz pkg chocolate chips
  • 1 6-oz pkg butterscotch chips

Instructions

Melt above ingredients in a saucepan.

ADD: 1 cup peanut butter and cool

Add to cooled mixture the following: 1 cup coconut 1 pkg colored mini marshmallows ½ cup finely chopped nuts

Mix together and pack in a buttered pan and keep in fridge.

item.title: Carrot & Raisin Salad
item.description:
Instructions

Combine in a mixing bowl 2 cups shredded carrots ½ cup diced celery ½ cup raisins

Mix together 1/3 cup mayonnaise or salad dressing 1 Tbps vinegar

Toss lightly with two forks and turn into lettuce-lined serving bowl.

Yield: 4 to 6 servings.

item.title: Carrot Casserole (barb Pierce)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 carrots, sliced (Boil 10 min.)
  • 1 medium onion
  • ¼ cup margarine
  • 1/8 cup flour
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1/6 tsp dry mustard
  • 1/8 tsp celery salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • grated cheese

Instructions

Cook onions in margarine in microwave for 5 min. Add flour, spices and mix. Add milk. Cook milk till thick 5 – 10 minutes. Layer carrots, onion-milk mixture, then cheese. Top with cracker crumbs, then cheese. Bake for 30 minutes at 350 degrees F.

item.title: Carrots and Pineapple (cw)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups baby carrots
  • 1 20-oz tin pineapple chunks
  • 4 tsp cornstarch
  • ½ tsp ground cinnamon
  • ½ cup packed brown sugar
  • 1 tbsp butter or margarine

Instructions

In a saucepan, bring 1” of water to a boil; place carrots in a steamer basket over water. Cover and steam for 8 – 10 minutes or until crisp-tender. Drain pineapple, reserving juice; set pineapple aside.

In a saucepan, combine cornstarch and cinnamon. Add the brown sugar, butter and reserved juice. Bring to a boil, cook and stir for 2 minutes or until thickened. Stir in the carrots and pineapple. Heat through. YIELD: 4 servings

item.title: Cauliflower Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp crushed garlic
  • 1 cup chopped onions
  • 1 medium cauliflower (separated into florets)
  • 4 cups chicken stock (broth)
  • 2 small potatoes, peeled and chopped
  • ¼ cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 2 Tbsp chopped fresh chives
  • In large non-stick saucepan, heat oil, brown the garlic and onions until softened (approx. 5 minutes). Add cauliflower, stock and potatoes. Bring to a boil, cover and reduce heat and simmer for 25 minutes or until tender.
  • Transfer to food processor and blend until creamy and smooth. Return to saucepan and thin with more stock of desired.
  • Serve with a sprinkle of cheese and chives.

item.title: Cavendish Farms Flavourcrisp Fries
item.description:
Ingredients

  • INGREDIENTS: 2 oz Mozzarella cheese, shredded
  • 1 oz onion, diced
  • 1 oz red pepper, diced
  • 1 oz green pepper, diced
  • DIPPING SAUCE:
  • ¼ cup mayonnaise
  • ¼ cup sour cream
  • 1 finely chopped garlic clove
  • 1 Tbsp chopped fresh dill

Instructions

Combine ingredients for dipping sauce and place in serving dish. Cook Cavendish Farms FlavourCrisp Crispy Coated or Spicy fries according to package directions. Combine the cheese, onions and peppers. Sprinkle over fries. Broil or bake at 450 degrees until the cheese is melted.

item.title: Charlie Brown Party Mix
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup butter or margarine
  • 1-1/4 tsp seasoned salt
  • 4-1/2 tsp Wooster sauce
  • 8 cups Chex cereals
  • 1 cup peanuts – of course!
  • 1 cup small pretzels

Instructions

Melt and mix seasonings in a large saucepan. Dump in remaining ingredients and mix thoroughly. 250 degree oven for 1 hour stirring well every 20 minutes.

item.title: Chassie’s Dills
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 13 cups water
  • 5 cups vinegar
  • 1 cup pickling salt
  • 1 tsp alum

Instructions

Bring to a boil. Put plenty of dill in jars and 2 garlic buds. Pour hot mixture over cukes to ½” from top of jar. Seal tightly, cool and store.

item.title: Cheesy Zucchini Bake (cw)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4-1/2 cups sliced zucchini
  • 2 – 3 tbsp oil salt & pepper to taste
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 tbsp minced garlic
  • 1 10-3/4 oz tin tomato puree
  • 1 6-oz tin tomato paste
  • 3 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tsp Italian seasoning
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • 2 2-1/4 oz tins of drained sliced ripe olives
  • 3 cups (12 oz) shredded mozzarella cheese 6 eggs, lightly beaten
  • 1-1/2 cups grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions

In a large skillet, sauté zucchini in oil until tender. Sprinkle with salt and pepper; stir; transfer to an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2 baking dish. In same skillet, sauté onion until crisp-tender. Add garlic; sauté 3 minutes longer. Stir in tomato puree, tomato paste, sugar, Italian seasoning and basil. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat. Simmer, uncovered, for 10-15 minutes or until slightly thickened. Stir in olives. Pour over zucchini. Sprinkle with mozzarella. Combine the eggs and Parmesan cheese; pour over zucchini. Bake, uncovered, at 375 degrees for 25-30 minutes or until a knife inserted near he center comes out clean. Let the dishstand for 15 minutes before serving. YIELD: 12 to 16 servings.

item.title: Chewy Chip Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs
  • 1 cup chocolate chips
  • 1 cup butterscotch chips
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts
  • 11-oz sweetened condensed milk

Instructions

Measure all ingredients. Stir together well. Grease a 9 x 9 pan. Smooth with back of spoon. Bake for 30 – 35 minutes in a 350 degree oven. Cool for 4 – 5 minutes. Cut into 36 squares

NOTE: I tried this in a well-greased pan and it was a real struggle to get out of the pan. The next time I will grease the pan, line it with tin foil and grease this very generously and keep my fingers crossed that it will come out easy.

item.title: Chicken Pie – (merit)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tin cream of chicken soup
  • 1 cup cubed cooked chicken
  • ½ cup milk or water
  • Mashed potatoes

Instructions

Place ingredients in a pan. Then cover with mashed potatoes. Heat in oven at 325 degrees for 30 minutes.

item.title: Chicken-veggie Stir-fry
item.description:
Instructions

Prep. Time 15 min. - Cooking Time – 20 min.

2 Tbsp cornstarch 1 lb skinless boneless chicken, cut into 1 tin condensed chicken broth strips 1 Tbsp soya sauce 1 onion, sliced ¼ tsp ground ginger 5 cups cut-up veggies (broccoli, carrots 2 Tbsp vegetable oil mushroom, celery & green onion)

Stir together cornstarch, broth, soya sauce and Ginger – set aside. In large skillet, heat half of the oil. Stir fry chicken with onion till browned. Remove from skillet; season lightly with salt and pepper and set aside. In same skillet, heat remaining oil. Stir fry veggies until tender crisp. Add reserved cornstarch mix. Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly. Return chicken to skillet. Heat thru. Serve over rice, if desired.

item.title: Chipits Crunchies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Melt 6-oz pig (1 cup) Chipits chocolate chips and 6-oz pkg (1 cup) Chipits butterscotch chips in a 1-1/2 quart bowl over hot (not boiling) water. Stir in 1 cup chow mein noodles and 1 cup peanuts.

Instructions

Drop with 2 teaspoons onto wax paper. Chill until firm. Makes 2 dozen.

For an interesting variation, substitute (for noodles) one cup broken thin pretzel sticks, corn flakes or rice crispies.

item.title: Chocolate Almond Bark
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Soda crackers
  • 1-1/2 cups chocolate chips
  • 1 cup butter
  • 1 cup chopped almonds
  • 1 cup brown sugar

Instructions

Grease cookie sheet. Line bottom of sheet with soda crackers. Boil butter and brown sugar for 5 minutes; keep stirring. Pour hot mixture over crackers. Bake in oven for 5 minutes at 375 degrees. Pour chocolate chips and almonds on top of hot crackers. Refrigerate to harden. Break into pieces. ENJOY!

item.title: Chocolate Chip Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 cups sifted flour
  • 1 cup chocolate chips
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Cream margarine well. Add sugar and vanilla and mix well. Add flour a little at a time and mix well. Then add chips and walnuts. Pack in a jelly roll pan and bake at 350 degree oven for 20-25 minutes. Cut while warm.

item.title: Chocolate Pizza - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8 oz white chocolate, divided
  • 8 oz semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • ½ cup each salted peanuts,
  • mini marshmallows,
  • crispy rice cereal,
  • coconut,
  • red & green cherries

Instructions

In a heavy saucepan or top or a double boiler, melt 6 oz white chocolate and all the chocolate chips. Stir in peanuts, marshmallows and cereal. Pour onto a greased 10”pizza pan or a 10” circle of cardboard covered with foil. Spread to even out top. Sprinkle with coconut. Top with cherries. Melt remaining white chocolate and drizzle over pizza. Chill.

item.title: Chocolate Rice Krispies Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup margarine
  • l/2 cup cocoa
  • 1/3 cup honey
  • 3 cups small marshmallows
  • 4 cups rice krispies
  • l cup peanuts (optional)

Instructions

Melt margarine in large pan, add honey, cocoa and marshmallows. Stir till marshmallows are melted. Remove from heat and add rice krispies and peanuts. Press into a buttered 9”square pan.

item.title: Chocolate Scotcharews
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 cup peanut butter
  • 1 cup corn syrup

Instructions

Mix and melt together in a large deep pan, then remove from stove. Add six cups Rice Krispies gradually and mix. Spread in a buttered 8” x 8”pan. Cool.

MELT TOGETHER;: 1 6-oz pkg chocolate chips 1 6-oz pkg butterscotch chips

Spread on top of above mixture and let cool. Cut in squares. Kids just love it.

item.title: Chocolate-caramel Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8-1/2 or 9-oz pkg cream-filled sugar wafer coolies
  • 24 vanilla caramels
  • 2 tbsp milk
  • 6-oz pkg (1 cup) semi-sweet choc.chips 3 tbsp margarine or butter

Instructions

Lightly grease 8”or 9” square pan. Arrange single layer of wafer cookies side by side over bottom of greased pan. Reserve half of wafer cookies for second layer. In small saucepan, melt caramels with milk over low heat, stirring occasionally until well blended.

item.title: Choke Cherry Wine
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 15 cups of cherries to 1 gallon of hot water
  • Acid Blend
  • 2 tsp per gallon
  • Grape Tannin
  • ¼ tsp per gallon
  • Pectic Enzyme
  • ½ tsp per gallon
  • Yeast Nutrient
  • ½ tsp per gallon
  • Sugar
  • 3 lbs per gallon - Stir well.
  • Wine Yeast
  • 1 pkg per 1 to 5 gallons

Instructions

Cool mixture and add 1 pkg of wine yeast. Stir daily for two weeks. After two weeks, extract liquid. NOTE: Use same cherries to make a second brew. (Make half the amount of first batch.)

item.title: Cocktail Sauce
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup ketchup
  • 1.5 tsp lemon juice
  • 1 Tbsp finely chopped onion
  • ¼ cup horseradish
  • Add Tabasco sauce, salt and pepper to taste

item.title: Cole Slaw Dressing
item.description:
Instructions

If you have a microwave and need a nice potato salad or cole slaw dressing in a hurry, here is one. In a medium glass bowl, with wire whip or rotary beater, thoroughly combine

½ cup water 1/3 cup vinegar ¼ to 1/3 cup sugar 1 egg 1 tbsp flour ½ tsp salt ½ tsp dry mustard

Heat on high 4 – 5 minutes until mixture is thickened, stirring twice; stir in

item.title: Coleslaw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 qts finely shredded cabbage
  • 1 tbsp coarse salt (salad salt)
  • 1 cup peeled and shredded carrot
  • ½ cup mayonnaise
  • ½ cup commercial sour cream
  • 1 tsp celery salt
  • 1 tbsp vinegar
  • Salt and pepper
  • 3 tsp sugar

Instructions

In a large colander over a large bowl, layer the shredded cabbage and coarse salt. Place a plate over the cabbage and weight it down with a heavy object. Allow cabbage to stand at room temperature for at least 2 hours. Press out as much moisture as possible and place cabbage and the shredded carrot in a large bowl. In a small bowl blend the mayonnaise, sour cream, celery salt, sugar and vinegar. Add the mayonnaise mixture to the cabbage and stir until well combined. Chill for several hours or overnight. Taste the coleslaw after it has been chilled and adjust the seasoning if necessary.

item.title: Colette’s Marshmallow Rolls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/3 cup small marshmallows
  • 3-1/4 cup graham crumbs
  • ½ cup red cherries (chopped)
  • ½ cup green cherries (chopped)
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts
  • 1 can Eagle Brand

Instructions

Mix well and roll into balls or shape into rolls for slicing.

item.title: Cooked Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 heaping tsp cornstarch
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 cup vinegar and 4 tsp water
  • 1 level tsp dry mustard
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 4 tbsp canned milk

Instructions

Combine above in double boiler and cook. After it starts to thicken, boil for 5 minutes. Chill before using.

item.title: Cornmeal and Herb Chicken Coating
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup dry bread crumbs
  • 1 cup powdered milk
  • 1 cup cornmeal
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 3 tbsp salt
  • 1 tbsp dried thyme
  • 1 tsp pepper
  • 1 tsp garlic salt
  • 1 tsp celery salt
  • 1 tsp onion salt
  • 1 tsp cried oregano

Instructions

Combine all the ingredients. To use, place 1 cup mixture in a plastic bag. Shake chicken pieces until coated. Place on a baking pan and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour. Store unused mixture in an airtight container.

item.title: Cranberry Chutney – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 16-oz package cranberries
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 cup orange juice
  • 1 cup raisins
  • 1 medium chopped granny smith apple
  • 1 Tbsp ground ginger
  • ¼ tsp ground cloves
  • ½ tsp ground nutmeg

Instructions

In a 3-quart saucepan, combine cranberries, water, raisins and shopped apple. Bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer. Cook, covered for 15 minutes or until cranberries pop.

Stir in sugar, orange juice, ginger, nutmeg and cloves. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.

item.title: Creamed Codfish – (from Metis Cookhouse by Gail Lebland_
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cooked codfish, fresh or frozen
  • 3 cups of milk
  • 4 Tbsp of butter or margarine
  • 4 Tbsp of flour

Instructions

Salt and pepper to taste

Melt butter or margarine in top of double boiler, blend in flour, add milk gradually and cook, stirring constantly until sauce thickens.

Add flaked fish. Add salt and pepper.

Serve on toast, or with hot baking powder biscuits or over broiled potatoes.

item.title: Crispy Chews
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup brown sugar
  • 2-1/2 cups Rice Krispies
  • 2//3 cup corn syrup
  • 1 cup coconut
  • 2/3 cup peanut butter
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Combine brown sugar, corn syrup and peanut butter over low heat. Remove and add remaining ingredients. Make small balls and put them on wax paper. Chill for ½ hour.

item.title: Crispy Topped Creamy Spinach (kraft What’s Cooking)
item.description:
Instructions

Prep: 10 min. Total: 35 minutes

2 pkg (300 g each) frozen chopped spinach, 1 tub (250 g) Philadelphia Chives & Onion thawed and well drained Spreadable Cream Cheese ½ cup Draft Rancher’s Choice Dressing 2 eggs, lightly beaten 1-1/2 cups Draft Double Cheddar Shreds, 1 cup crushed Ritz Crackers, divided divided

PREHEAT oven to 375 F. Mix spinach, cream cheese spread, dressing, eggs and ¾ cup of the shredded cheese in large bowl. Stir in ½ cup of the crushed crackers.

item.title: Crumb Coating
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups dry sifted toasted breadcrumbs
  • oil for brushing
  • ½ cup flour
  • 2 tsp M.S.G. (Accent)
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ tsp fruit sugar
  • ¼ to ½ tsp garlic salt
  • ½ tsp flavor salt

Instructions

To make the crumb coating brush sparingly with oil (or melted butter) the slices from half a standard loaf of bread. Arrange overlapping a little on broiler pan and dry out until crisp in oven at 200 degrees F. Buzz in blender until extremely fine. To the two cups add all remaining crumb-coating ingredients and mix well.

item.title: Crunchy Fudge Sandwiches
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 6-oz pkg (1 cup) Butterscotch Morsels 1 6-oz (1 cup) Chocolate Chips
  • ½ cup peanut butter
  • ½ cup sifted icing sugar
  • 4 cups Rice Krispies
  • 2 tbsp soft butter or margarine
  • 1 tbsp water

Instructions

Melt Butterscotch Morsels with peanut butter in heavy saucepan over very low heat, stirring until well blended. Remove from heat. Add Rice Krispies; stir until well coated with butterscotch mixture. Press half of cereal mixture into buttered 8 x 8 x 2 pan. Chill in refrigerator while preparing fudge mixture. Set remaining cereal mixture aside. Combine chocolate chips, sugar, butter and water in top of double boiler; place over hot water and stir until chocolate melts and mixture is well blended. Spread over chilled cereal mixture. Spread remaining cereal mixture evenly over top. Press in gently. Chill. Remove from refrigerator for about 10 minutes before cutting into squares.

item.title: Dandy Dog Bone Biscuits
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups whole wheat flour
  • 2 cups cornmeal
  • l cup parmesan or cheddar cheese
  • l/2 cup water or broth
  • ¾ cup vegetable oil, drained tuna oil or bacon/chicken fat

Instructions

Preheat oven to 300 degrees. Combine flour, cornmeal and cheese in a large bowl. Stir in oil until mixture is crumbly. Stir in water/broth until well mixed. Roll dough on lightly floured surface to a thickness of l/2”. Biscuits can be cut out with a 2”or 4”dog bone cookie cutter. Transfer to an ungreased baking sheet. Bake for 45 minutes. Transfer to rack to cool completely.

item.title: Date Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 cups rolled oats
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup coconut
  • 1-1/4 cup shortening
  • ½ tsp soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1-1/2 lb dates

Instructions

Rub first 8 ingredients together until crumbly. Put 2/3 mixture in bottom of a 9 x 12 cake pan. Cook dates with 1-1/2 cups water until soft. Add ¼ to ½ cup white sugar and 2 tbsp butter. Mix well. Spread date mixture over crumbs in bottom of pan. Top with remaining crumb mixture. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.

item.title: Do-das
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg. marshmallows
  • 1 small tin Carnation milk
  • Graham wafers or Graham wafer crumbs

Instructions

Soak marshmallows in the milk for 3 to 4 hours. Stir often without breaking them up. Roll out graham wafers to a powder. Drop one marshmallow at a time on or into the graham wafer crumbs and roll. Place on a plate to dry.

item.title: Dumplings (made with Bisquick)
item.description:
Instructions

Mix 2 cups Bisquick, ¾ cup milk well with fork. Drop by tablespoonfuls gently onto boiling stew. Cook over low heat 10 minutes uncovered and 10 minutes covered. Let stew bubble gently.

Makes 10 – 12 dumplings.

item.title: Dunkin’ Crunchin’ Dip
item.description:
Instructions

In a bowl, whisk together one 35 gr packet of Old El Paso Seasoning Mix, 1 cup of light sour cream and ½ cup of light mayonnaise until thoroughly combined.

Stir in 2/3 cup shredded Monterey Jack cheese.

Service with vegetable sticks and chicken wings.

Makes 2-1/2 cups

item.title: Eagle Brand Milk Substitute
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup boiling water
  • 3 tbsp margarine
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1-1/2 cup instant powdered milk

Instructions

Put in blender and mix. No cooking. Can be used for all recipes that call for Eagle Brand milk.

Eagle Brand has a measure of 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons in a 15 oz can.

item.title: Easiest Ever Lemon Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup granulated sugar
  • ½ cup butter
  • 1/3 cup raspberry jam (or your choice)
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 pkg (212 g) Shirriff Lemon Pie Filling
  • ½ cup ground almonds
  • ¼ cup sliced almonds

Instructions

Combine flour and sugar, then butter until the mixture clumps together. Press firmly in 8” square pan. Bake at 350 degrees 15 – 18 minutes or until slightly browned. Remove from oven and spread with jam. Beat eggs until light and creamy. Beat in pie filling and ground almonds. Pour over jam layer. Sprinkle with sliced almonds. Bake 20 –25 minutes or until set and firm to touch. Cool. Cut into squares. Makes 25 squares.

item.title: Easiest Ever Peanut Butter Treats (kraft What’s Cooking)
item.description:
Instructions

No –Bake Double Peanut Butter Bars Prep: 20 Min. Total: 20 Min.

1-1/2 cup Draft Smooth Peanut Butter 2/3 cup honey 2/3 cup sugar 3 cups Post Honey Bunches of Oats Cereal 1 cup peanuts 4 squares Baker’s Semi-Sweet Chocolate, melted.

MICROWAVE peanut butter, honey and sugar in large microwaveable bowl on HIGH 2 or 3 min. or until sugar is melted and mixture is smooth, stirring every minute.

item.title: Easy Fudge Recipe
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ¼ cup corn syrup
  • ¼ cup melted butter
  • 2 tbsp cocoa

Instructions

Mix in small saucepan. Bring to a boil (soft ball stage). Five minutes after it begins to boil, remove from heat. Add vanilla and nuts. Less cocoa will give a caramel fudge.

item.title: Easy Peach-pear Salsa – (nugget Thursday Food Page)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2.5 l (10 cups) coarsely chopped peeled peaches
  • 1.25 l (5 cups) chopped peeled pears
  • (about) 3 kg/6-1/2 lbs)
  • (about 1.2kg/2-3/4 lbs)
  • 4 limes
  • 60 to 90 ml (4 to 6 Tbsp) liquid honey
  • 1 – 115 g pkg commercial salsa mix

Instructions

PREPARATION: Place 6 clean 500-ml (1 cup) Mason jars on a rack in a boiling water canner; cover jars with water to heat to a simmer (82C/180F). Set screw bands aside. Heat snap lid sealing discs in hot water, not boiling (82C/180F). Keep jars & sealing discs hot until ready to use. 2. In a large, deep stainless-steel saucepan, combine peaches and pears. Grate peel from limes and squeeze juice. Add grated peel, 125 ml (1/2 cup) lime juice, honey and salsa mix to fruit. Stirring continuously until well mixed, bring mixture to a full rolling boil. Cook and stir for 2 minutes. 3. Ladle hot salsa into a hot jar to within 1cm (1/2”) of top of jar (headspace). Using a non-metallic utensil, remove all bubbles and adjust headspace, if required, by adding more salsa. Wipe jar rim, removing any food residue. Centre hot sealing disc on clean jar rim. Screw band down until resistance is met, then increase to finger tip tight. Return filled jar to rack in canner. Repeat for remaining salsa. 4. When canner is filled, ensure that all jars are covered by at least 2.5 cm (1”) of water. Cover canner and bring water to a full rolling boil before starting to count processing time. At altitudes of up to 305 m (1000 ft), process (boil filled jars) for 15 minutes for 250-ml (1-cup) jars, 20 minutes for 500-ml (2-cup) jars. 5. When processing time is complete, remove canner lid, wait for 5 minutes, then remove jars without tilting and place them upright on a protected work surface. Let cool upright, undisturbed, for 24 hours; do not retighten screw bands. 6. After cooling, check jar seals. Sealed discs curve downward and do not move when pressed. Remove screw bands; wipe and dry bands and jars. Store screw bands separately or replace loosely on jars, as desired. Label and store jars in a cool, dark place. For best quality, use home-canned foods within one year. Makes about 6 500-ml (2-cup) or 12 250-ml (1-cup) jars. Source: Bernadin

item.title: Edna Nadon’s Mustard Beans
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lb (about 8 cups) yellow beans, cut in 1” pieces
  • ½ cup flour
  • 1-1/2 tsp salt
  • ¼ cup dry mustard
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 tsp turmeric
  • 1 Tbsp celery seed
  • 3 cups Heinz white vinegar
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Cook beans in lightly salted water until just tender-crisp; drain. In large heavy saucepan, mix flour, mustard, turmeric, salt, sugar and celery seed. Stir in vinegar and water gradually. Bring to a boil over low heat and cook, stirring constantly until thickened, about 3 to 4 minutes. Add beans to sauce and bring to boiling. Pack into hot, sterilized jars and seal.

item.title: Emergency Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • (Tastes lie Kraft Catalina)
  • 1 tsp salt
  • l tsp paprika
  • l/3 cup sugar
  • 2 tbsp lemon juice
  • l/4 cup vinegar
  • 2/3 cup salad oil
  • l/3 cup catsup (ketchup)

Instructions

Measure ingredients into bottle or jar. Cover tightly and shake until well mixed. Makes l-l/4 cups.

NOTE: Catsup is easily measured if you measure the oil then add the catsup to make l cup. When measured this way, the catsup doesn’t stick to the measuring cup.

item.title: Fabulously Flawless Chocolate Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8 oz (8 squares) Baker’s semi-sweet chocolate
  • 2/3 cup sweetened condensed milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup chopped nuts (optional)

Instructions

Melt chocolate with milk over hot water. Remove from heat; blend in vanilla, salt and nuts. Spread in buttered 8”x 4”loaf pan. Flatten with buttered spoon. Chill until firm, then cut into squares. Makes 18 candies.

NOTE: Recipe may be doubled and spread in 8-inch square pan.

item.title: Festive Punch
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 qt club soda
  • 1 6-oz can pink lemonade
  • 1 6-oz can orange juice
  • 4 cups cranberry juice

Instructions

combine all ingredients and chill.

item.title: Fluffy Potato Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups hot or cold mashed potatoes
  • 1 small onion, finely chopped
  • 1 8-oz pkg cream cheese, softened
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 Tbsp all-purpose flour
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Put the potatoes into the large bowl of your electric mixer. Add the cream cheese, chopped onion, eggs, and flour. Beat at medium speed until the ingredients are blended, then bet at high speed until light and fluffy. Taste and add salt and pepper, if needed. Spoon into a greased 9-inch square baking dish. Bake, uncovered, in a 300 degree oven for about 35 minutes.

item.title: Freezer Pickles
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 7 cups sliced pickling cucumber, with peel
  • 1 large onion, sliced
  • 1 cup thinly sliced peppers, seeded, all green or half red
  • 1 Tbsp coarse pickling salt
  • ½ tsp celery seed
  • 1 cup white vinegar
  • 2 cups granulated sugar

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in bowl. Stir well. Cover. Refrigerate for 3 days. Stir every day. Pack into freezer containers, covering with brine to within 1 inch of top. Freeze. Thaw before serving. Keeps in refrigerator at least 8 weeks. Keeps in freezer at least 1 year. Makes 3 pints.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Dolly Drops
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 Tbsp Crisco
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 3 Tbsp water

Instructions

Cook 3 minutes. Dip pieces of bread in mixture (cut 2” or 3” pieces) and place on wax paper.

Sprinkle with coconut.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Never Fail Hot Water Partry
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups flour
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 lb Tenderflake lard
  • 1 cup boiling water

Instructions

Combine dry ingredients. Cut lard into it until grainy. Add water. Mix thoroughly. Wrap and set 24 hours. Take out 6 hours before use. Will last 3 months in fridge, 6 months in freezer.

Makes 4 2-crust pies.

NOTE: originated in England approx. 150 years ago. If you want to make it fussy for fruit pies, roll top crust in sugar.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Sugar Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 Tbsp cream or milk
  • 3 Tbsp melted butter
  • 1 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Mix all together. Then place in uncooked tart shells and bake at 400 F. until pie is golden brown.

item.title: Frozen Pops
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg (3 oz) Jell-O Gelatin (any flavor)
  • 1 envelope Kool-Aid (any flavour)
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 2 cups boiling water
  • 2 cups cold water

Instructions

Dissolve Jell-O Gelatin, instant soft drink mix and sugar in boiling water. Add cold water. Pour into ice cube trays, small paper cups, or frozen pop molds. Insert wooden sticks or paper spoons diagonally in each ice cube section or at an angle in colds or cups for handles. (If desired, pops may be partially frozen before handles are inserted.) Freeze until firm – 2 to 3 hours. Makes 20 to 24 pops.

item.title: Fruit Pizza
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Variety of Fruit
  • Topping:
  • Crust:
  • 12 oz cream cheese, softened
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup granulated sugar
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ¼ cup icing sugar
  • Apricot Glaze:
  • 1 cup butter, softened
  • ¼ cup apricot jam or orange marmalade
  • 1 Tbsp water

Instructions

Crust: Mix all ingredients together in a bowl until it forms a ball. Press onto bottom of pizza pan. Bake at 350 degree F about 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Cool

item.title: Fruity Bread Pudding for Two
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup cubed bread
  • ½ cup chopped peeled apples
  • ¼ cup dried cranberries, cherries or apricots
  • 1 egg
  • ¼ cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1 tsp butter
  • Cinnamon

Instructions

In lightly greased 2-cup baking dish, toss together bread, apples and cranberries.

In small bowl, whisk together egg, sugar and vanilla. In small saucepan, heat milk with butter just until steaming; gradually whisk into egg mixture in slow, steady stream. Pour over bread mixture. Let stand for 5 minutes, press gently to loisten bread evenly. Sprinkle with cinnamon to taste.

item.title: Garden Vegetable Stir-fry for One – (good Times)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup vegetable, chicken or beef stock
  • 1/3 cup couscous
  • 2 tsp vegetable oil
  • ¼ cup chopped onion
  • 2 tsp minced gingerroot
  • 2 cups packed fresh spinach, chopped
  • ½ cup each diced sweet red and green peppers
  • ½ cup sugar snap peas, trimmed
  • 2 tsp soya sauce
  • ¼ cup sliced radishes
  • Pinch each salt and pepper

Instructions

In saucepan, bring stock to boil. Add couscous; cover and remove from heat. Let stand for 5 minutes; fluff with fork.

item.title: Glazed Berry Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 baked 9- or 10-inch pie shell
  • 5 cups blackberries, strawberries, raspberries
  • 3 tbsp cornstarch
  • or blueberries
  • 1 cup sugar
  • ¾ cup water
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • 1 cup whipping cream

Instructions

Crush and sieve 1 cup of the berries to remove seeds. Combine cornstarch, sugar, sieved berries, and water. Cook over medium-low heat until thickened and clear, about 10 minutes. Cool slightly, then add lemon juice and remaining berries. Mix lightly and pile into baked pie shell. Cool and chill about 2 hours. At serving time, whip the cream and pipe or spoon around the rim.

item.title: Golden Graham Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup light corn syrup
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 tsp margarine or butter
  • 1 10-oz pkg golden grahams
  • 1 11-oz pkg chocolate chips
  • 3 cup mini marshmallows

Instructions

Butter 13”x 9”x 2” baking pan. Heat syrup, butter and chocolate chips just to boiling in a 3-quart saucepan, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Pour over cereal in large bowl; toss quickly until coated. Fold in marshmallows, 1 cup at a time. Press in pan. Let stand until firm – about 1 hour.

item.title: Golden Grahams S’mores
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup light corn syrup
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 3 Tbsp margarine
  • 9 cups Golden Grahams cereal (1-12 oz box)
  • 1 pkg (11.5 oz) milk chocolate chips
  • 3 cups miniature marshmallows

Instructions

Grease rectangular pan, 13 x 9 x 2”. Heat corn syrup, margarine and chocolate chips to boiling in 3-quart saucepan, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Pour over cereal in large bowl. Toss until completely coated with chocolate. Fold in marshmallows, 1 cup at a time. Press mixture evenly in pan with buttered back of spoon. Let stand until firm, at least 1 hour. Cut into 2-inch squares. Store loosely covered at room temperature no longer than 2 days. Makes 24 squares.

item.title: Grandma’s Molasses Cookies
item.description:
Instructions

Mix together ½ cup softened butter ½ cup white sugar 1 cup molasses Add 2 tsp baking soda 2 tsp ginger 2-1/2 cups white flour

Mix well then shape i8nto small balls and press down with a fork. Add candy decorations if you wish. Bake in 350 F. Makes 3 dozen cookies.

item.title: Greek Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ head lettuce, torn bite size
  • 1 Romaine lettuce
  • 2 tomatoes, cut bite size
  • 1 cucumber, cut bite size
  • ¼ cup sliced green onions
  • ¼ cup sliced ripe olives
  • ¾ cup Feta cheese, cubed
  • ½ cup salad oil
  • ¼ cup red wine vinegar
  • ¼ cup chopped parsley
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 1/8 tsp oregano
  • 1/8 tsp garlic powder

Instructions

Layer lettuce in large salad bowl or on a large platter. Spread with tomatoes, cucumber, onions, olives and cheese. Beat next seven ingredients together well. Shake before serving, Serve with salad.

item.title: Green Tomato Ketchup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Louise Beaudry’s Grandmother’s recipe
  • 5 cups brown sugar
  • 8 onions, sliced (5 cups)
  • 34 cups (24) lg green tomatoes, sliced
  • 4 cups white vinegar
  • 2 cups water
  • 4 tbsp pickling spice

Instructions

Soak tomato and onion slices overnight in 1 cup of coarse salt and 12 cups water. In the morning, rinse tomato and onion slices well. Add brown sugar, vinegar, water and cheese cloth bag of pickling spices. Boil gently until tomato slices start to fall apart. Pour hot into sterilized jars and seal.

item.title: Hallowe’en Fudge – Makes 48 Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8 oz. Cream cheese, softened
  • 16 oz powdered sugar
  • 2 pkg (6 squares each white baking
  • 1-1/2 tsp vanilla
  • chocolate squares, melted
  • 1 cup toasted chopped nuts
  • 8 drops yellow food coloring
  • 4 drops red food coloring
  • multi-colored sprinkles

Instructions

Beat cream cheese in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed until smooth. Gradually add sugar, beating until well blended. Stir in white chocolate, vanilla, nuts and food coloring until well mixed. Spread on foil-lined 8” square pan. Top with sprinkles Refrigerate 1 hour or until firm. Cut into squares.

item.title: Herbed Lemon Marinade – for Fish, Steaks, Scallops or Shrimp
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ cup Realemon lemon juice
  • ¼ cup chopped green onions
  • 1 Tbsp water
  • ½ tsp garlic salt
  • ¼ to ½ tsp dried dill weed

Instructions

In small bow, combine oil, lemon juice, green onions, water, garlic salt and dill weed. Pour over fish, steaks, scallops or shrimp. Cover and marinate 1 hour. Remove fish from marinade. Grill or broil as desired, basting frequently with marinade. Makes about ¾ cup.

item.title: Homemade Soap – (from the Hazelwood Cookbook)
item.description:
Instructions

Place in large crock: 6 cups soft water

Into this put: 1 tin lye ½ lb Borax

When completely dissolved, put in 9 cups lukewarm fat, constantly stirring with a wooden stick until mixture begins to thicken. Set away in a warm place until next day, then cut into bars. Let ripen for 3 weeks before using.

item.title: Honey Garlic Marinade
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 7 ml Canola Gold Oil
  • 25 ml light soy sauce
  • 50 ml liquid honey
  • 2 cloves garlic

Instructions

Combine Canola Gold, soy sauce, honey and garlic in a small bowl. Mix well.

Marinade for 2 to 4 hours in the refrigerator. Excellent for chicken, turkey or pork. While cooking, turn frequently to avoid burning.

item.title: Honey-maple Popcorn Balls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup liquid honey
  • ¼ cup each of water or maple syrup
  • 7 to 8 cups air-popped popcorn

Instructions

Place honey, water and maple syrup in a medium-size saucepan set over medium-high heat. Boil, uncovered, without stirring, until thick, about 8 to 9 minutes. Meanwhile, place popcorn in a large bowl. Pour syrup over popcorn. Toss using 1 or 2 large spoons until popcorn is evenly coated. Using lightly buttered hands, form popcorn into 10 3” balls. Gobble up right away or wrap in waxed paper and store at room temperature.

item.title: Honey-mustard Carrots (cw)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cups sliced or baby carrots
  • 2 tbsp oil
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup
  • 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
  • 2 tsp dried parsley flakes

Instructions

In a saucepan, bring 1” of water to a boil. Place carrots in a steamer basket over water. Cover and steam for 8 – 10 minutes or until crisp-tender.

In a saucepan, combine the oil, honey, syrup and mustard; bring to a boil. Stir in carrots and parsley.

item.title: Hot Chocolate Mix - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 box (8 qts) non-fat dry milk
  • 1 6-oz non-dairy coffee creamer
  • 1 16-oz container dry chocolate
  • ½ cup confectioners’sugar
  • milk mix

Instructions

Place all ingredients in a very large bowl or kettle. Stir until well blended. Store in airtight containers or pack into small gift containers. To serve, add ¼ cup chocolate mix to 2/3 cup hot water.

item.title: Impossible Bacon Breakfast Pie (quiche)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb bacon
  • l can green chili (chopped)
  • l cup grated cheese (Jack)
  • l-l/2 cups milk
  • l cup Bisquick
  • 6 oz sour cream
  • 3 eggs
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Brown, drain and crumble bacon. Put in bottom of ll”x 7”greased dish. Top with onion and cheese. Pour rest in blender for l minute. Pour into pan. Bake at 350 degrees until tests done with a knife. (35 – 45 minutes.)

item.title: Impossible Breakfast Quiche
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup Bisquick
  • Dash pepper
  • ½ cup butter – melted
  • ½ cup sausage cooked and crumbled
  • 1-1/2 cups milk

Instructions

Blend all but cheese and meat for 15 seconds in blender. Sprinkle with cheese and meat on top. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Cool for 5 minutes.

item.title: Impossible Chicken ‘n Broccoli Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 10-oz pkg frozen chopped broccoli
  • 1-1/3 cup shredded Cheddar Cheese 8 oz.
  • 1-1/2 cup cooked chopped chicken
  • 2/3 cup chopped onion
  • 1-1/3 cup milk
  • 3 eggs
  • ¾ cup Bisquick
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate 10 x 1-1/2”. Rinse broccoli in cold water to thaw; drain thoroughly. Mix broccoli, cheese, the chicken and onion in pie plate. Beat milk, eggs, baking mix, salt and pepper 15 seconds in blender on high speed, 1 minute with wire whisk or hand beater or until smooth. Pour into pie plate. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes.

item.title: Impossible Chicken Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cut-up chicken
  • ½ - 1 can mushrooms, drained
  • ½ cup diced green onions
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 cup shredded Swiss or brick cheese
  • 1-1/2 cup milk
  • ¾ cup Bisquick

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate and sprinkle with flour. Into pie plate, place the chicken, mushrooms, onions, salt and cheese. Beat remaining ingredients until smooth. Pour over mixture in pie plate. Bake until knife is clean when inserted into center of pie – 30 to 35 minutes. If you wish you can sprinkle top of pie with Parmesan cheese or corn flake crumbs.

item.title: Impossible Coconut Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups milk
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • ½ cup biscuit mix
  • 4 eggs
  • ¼ cup butter or margarine
  • 1-1/2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup flaked coconut

Instructions

Combine milk, sugar, biscuit mix, eggs, butter and vanilla in electric blender container. Cover and blend on low speed for 3 minutes. Pour into greased pie plate. Let stand about 5 minutes, then sprinkle with coconut. Bake at 350 degrees for 40 minutes. Serve warm or cold.

item.title: Impossible French Apple Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups sliced pared tart apples
  • ¾ cup milk
  • 1-1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
  • ½ cup Bisquick
  • ¼ tsp ground nutmeg
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 2 tbsp soft margarine or butter
  • Streusel (Below)

Instructions

Heat oven to 325 degrees. Grease pie plate 10xl-l/2”. Mix apples and spices, turn into plate. Beat remaining ingredients except Streusel until smooth, 15 seconds in blender on high or l minute with hand beater. Pour into plate. Sprinkle with Streusel. Bake until knife inserted in center comes out clean, 55 to 60 minutes. STREUSEL;Mix 1 cup Bisquick baking mix, ½ cup chopped nuts, 1/3 cup packed brown sugar and 3 tbsp firm margarine or butter until crumbly.

item.title: Impossible Ham ‘n Cheese Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cut-up fully cooked smoked ham
  • 4 eggs
  • 1 cup shredded natural Swiss cheese
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1/3 cup chopped green onion or onion
  • 1 cup Bisquick
  • ¼ tsp salt, if desired
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate, 10 x 1-1/2”. Sprinkle ham, cheese and onion in plate. Beat remaining ingredients until smooth, 15 seconds in blender on high or 1 minute with hand beater. Pour into plate. Bake until golden brown and knife inserted in center comes out clean, 35 to 40 minutes. Cool 5 minutes. 6 servings.

item.title: Impossible Lasagne Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup creamed cottage cheese
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp dried oregano
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 can (6 oz) tomato paste
  • 2 eggs
  • ½ cup tea biscuit mix

Instructions

Spread cottage cheese in greased 9” pie plate. Cook ground beef, drain. Stir in ½ cup mozzarella cheese, salt, oregano and tomato paste. Spoon over cottage cheese. Stir milk, eggs and biscuit mix with fork until blended. Pour into pie plate. Bake 30-35 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake 1 – 2 minutes more until cheese is melted. What could be simpler? And follow this up with a dessert that’s just as easy. IMPOSSIBLE PUMPKIN PIE1

item.title: Impossible Pumpkin Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 can (16 oz) pumpkin
  • ½ cup tea biscuit mix
  • 2-1/2 tsp pumpkin pie spice
  • 2 tbsp margarine
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 can evaporated milk
  • 3 eggs

Instructions

Put everything in a blender. Mix well. Grease a 9”pie plate. Fill. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 45 – 50 minutes.

item.title: Jason’s Doggie Biscuits
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cups unbleached flour
  • 2 cups whole wheat flour
  • l cup rye flour
  • 2 cups bulgar (cracked wheat)
  • l cup cornmeal
  • l/2 cup instant nonfat dry milk powder
  • 4 tsp salt
  • l tbsp (l envelope) dry yeast
  • l/4 cup warm water
  • 3 cups chicken broth
  • l egg, slightly beaten with l sp milk

Instructions

Heat oven to 300. Mix the first 7 dry ingredients with a wooden spoon in a large bowl. Dissolve yeast thoroughly in warm water in glass measuring cup. Add to dry ingredients. Add chicken broth to flour mixture. Stir until dough forms. Roll our dough until it is l/4”thick. Using a large dog biscuit for model, cut out bone shapes from dough. Place on prepared cookie sheets. Brush dough with egg glaze. Bake bones for 45 minutes. Turn oven off. Biscuits should remain in oven overnight to harden.

item.title: Judy Lafreniere’s Punch
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can pink lemonade
  • ½ bottle cranberry juice
  • ½ can pineapple juice
  • 1 bottle gingerale
  • Wine or champagne (optional)

item.title: Judy’s Miracle Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 cups water
  • 2 28-oz tins tomatoes
  • 2 green peppers, diced
  • 1 medium cabbage, shredded
  • 1 large onion, finely chopped
  • 1 stalk celery, diced
  • 1 35-gram pkg onion soup mix

Instructions

In large pot, combine water, tomatoes, peppers, cabbage, onion, celery and onion soup mix. Bring to boil, reduce heat and simmer 45 minutes, adding more water if desired. Makes about 24 cups.

item.title: Kellogg’s Crispix Recipe #3
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups Kellogg’s Crispix Cereal
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 5 tsp Club House SALADS ‘N DIPS
  • Dressing & Dip Mix - approx ½ pkg – any variety

Instructions

In large mixing bowl, using two spatulas, gently toss cereal with oil until well coated. Spread on cookie sheet.

Bake at 250 degrees for 20 minutes, stirring after 10 minutes. Remove from oven.

Place dry mix in 8-cup or larger container. Add warm cereal, close container securely and gently shake and turn until cereal is thoroughly coated with mix. Serve warm, or cool on tray and store in container. Yield: 6 cups.

item.title: Knox Blox
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 envelopes Knox Unflavoured Gelatine
  • 3 pkgs. (3 oz each) flavoured gelatine
  • 4 cups boiling water

Instructions

In a large bow, combine Knox Unflavoured Gelatine and flavoured gelatine; add boiling water and stir until gelatine dissolves. Pour into jelly roll pan (l5-1/2 “ x 10-1/2”) or large shallow baking pan. Chill until firm. Using cookie cutters, cut out shapes and remove carefully. If desired, decorate with glossy decorating gel (available in 11/16 oz tubes) or with chocolate or multi-coloured long sprinkles as pictures. (Note:- other varieties of sprinkles may melt – test before using.)

item.title: Korny Spuds
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Potatoes, peeled
  • Corn flakes
  • l-l/2 tsp paprika
  • 2 tsp salt
  • Bacon fat

Instructions

Crush corn flakes and mix with paprika and salt. Cut peeled potatoes into serving size pieces, roll in melted bacon fat, then roll in corn flake mixture. Place in a greased covered casserole and bake in a 350 degree oven for l hour. This is delicious and different when you are tired of boiled, mashed or baked potatoes. Be sure to prepare enough – the family will eat twice as many prepared this way.

item.title: Kote-n-roast
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 12 slices white bread
  • Butter (see below)
  • ¼ cup dried red & green sweet pepper flakes
  • ¼ cup parsley flakes
  • 2 tsp cornmeal
  • ½ tsp onion salt
  • ½ tsp celery seed
  • ½ tsp paprika
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 1 tbsp whole wheat flour (optional)
  • 2 tsp flavor salt
  • 1 tbsp grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions

Butter bread on one side, arrange, buttered side up, on large cookie sheets and toast at 250 F. until golden and crisp (about 1 hour). Buzz toasted bread slices, pepper flakes and parsley in blender until as fine as possible. In bowl combine thoroughly the toasted buzzed crumbs with all remaining ingredients. It is now ready to use. NOTE: Leftover coating should be stored in plastic bag or tightly covered Tupperware container in refrigerator. If you want to use this coating on chicken legs, thighs or breasts, do not dry them. Shake on coating and place on buttered shallow pan and bake at 325 F. for 60 to 70 minutes. OR Bake at 350 F. for 45 minutes turning chicken pieces over once.

item.title: Lemon Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ cup icing sugar

Instructions

Mix until all crumbly. Lightly grease a 9”x 13” pan. Press in pan and bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees. (Looks watery).

Topping:

4 eggs 2 cups white sugar ½ tsp salt 1 tsp baking powder 4 tbsp lemon juice

Mix (not cook) – pour on crust and bake 18 minutes at 350 degrees.

item.title: Lemon Squares - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup butter or margarine, softened
  • ¼ cup confectioners’ sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • ½ tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 Tbsp lemon juice

Instructions

Heat oven to 350 F. Mix thoroughly flour, butter and confectioners’ sugar. Press in ungreaased square pan, 8x8x2 inches building up ½” edge. Bake 20 minutes.

Beat remaining ingredients about 3 minutes or until light and fluffy. Pour over hot crust.

item.title: Lena Langevin Creamy Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 medium potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 1 small onion, chopped
  • 2 Tbsp flour
  • 1 cup milk
  • Celery salt and garlic to taste (optional)
  • 2 slices processed cheese

Instructions

Boil potatoes with seasonings in enough water to cover, until cooked. Mash potatoes with their cooking water. They should be slightly liquefied. In small saucepan, melt butter. Add flour to form a smooth paste. Stir butter mixture into mashed potatoes. Over low heat, gradually stir milk into potato mixture. Add cheese; stir until melted. Makes 3 to 4 servings.

item.title: Liz’s Cabbage Slaw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups shredded cabbage
  • 1 cup shredded carrot
  • ¼ cup chopped green pepper
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 2 Tbsp sugar
  • 2 Tbsp vinegar
  • 1 tsp prepared mustard
  • 1 tsp celery seed
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Combine veggies and chill. Mix with slaw mix and toss well.

Serves 12.

item.title: Low-cal Cajun Corn
item.description:
Instructions

Mix together: 1 tsp garlic powder ½ tsp dried oregano ½ tsp dried thyme ½ tsp dried lemon peel ¼ tsp slt Pinch cayenne Pinch white pepper

Pop ½ cup popping corn

Melt 2 Tbsp butter

Pour melted butter over popped corn and toss until well coated. Sprinkle mixed seasoning over corn and toss again until well coated.

Makes 12 – 16 cups – 35 cal. Per cup

item.title: M.n.r. Sugar Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup brown sugar
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup flour
  • 1 pie shell, uncooked
  • 1-1/4 cup cream

Instructions

Mix all ingredients. Pour in pie crust. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake another 25 – 30 minutes until filling is set. Cool before serving

Tastes great with scoop of ice cream.

item.title: Magic French Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups (one 500-gr pkg) semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • 1 can Eagle Brand Milk
  • Dash salt
  • 1-1/2 tsp vanilla extract
  • ½ cup chopped nuts, optional

Instructions

In top of double boiler, melt chocolate over boiling water, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat, stir in sweetened condensed milk, salt, vanilla and nuts. Spread mixture evenly onto waxed paper lined 8 x 8” baking pan. Chill 2 hours or until firm. Turn fudge onto cutting board. Peel off paper and cut into squares. Tightly cover any leftovers.

item.title: Main Course Cabbage Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 – 28 oz can tomatoes
  • 1 medium onion
  • 2 – 5-1/2 oz cans tomato paste
  • ½ cup diced celery
  • 6 cups hot water
  • ½ green pepper, chopped
  • 2 beef or chicken bouillon cubes
  • 2 tbsp oil
  • 3 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 2 tsp salt
  • 2 cups raw potatoes, diced
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 1 cup raw carrot, diced
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • ¼ tsp baking soda
  • 6 cups cabbage, coarsely chopped

Instructions

In a very large soup pot, sauté ground beef, onion, celery and green pepper in the oil until redness is gone from meat. Break meat up as it sautés. Add remaining ingredients, except cabbage, and combine thoroughly. Simmer uncovered for 1 hr, stirring occasionally. Add cabbage and simmer covered, for 1 hr. longer. If soup is too thick, more water may be added.

item.title: Malva Pudding (claire Massicotte)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • CAKE: 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tbsp apricot jam
  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • generous pinch of salt
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp vinegar
  • 1 cup milk
  • SAUCE 1 cup cream
  • 6 oz butter
  • 1 cup sugar
  • ½ cup hot water

Instructions

Beat the egg and sugar well in a mixer. Add the jam, sift flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Melt the butter and add the vinegar. Add liquids to egg mixture alternately with the flour. Beat well and bake in a covered dish at 350 degrees F. for 45 minute to 1 hour. It is important to use a covered dish otherwise sauce won’t sink into the cake.

item.title: Maple Butter Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 recipe pastry
  • 1/3 cup butter or margarine
  • ½ cup maple syrup
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup raisins or walnuts
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten

Instructions

Prepare pastry. Line medium tart tins. Combine remaining ingredients. Mix well. Fill tart tins 2/3 full. Bake at 350 degrees for about 25-30 minutes. Makes about 16 medium tarts.

item.title: Maple Creamy Fondue
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup pure maple syrup
  • 2 cups 15% cream
  • 2 tsp cornstarch
  • firm fruits and berries cut into pieces

Instructions

Gently heat maple syrup in a pan for 5 minutes. Dilute the cornstarch in 2 tsp of cream. Bring the remaining cream to a boil and add to the maple syrup. Blend the cornstarch into the syrup mixture and stir continuously over gentle heat until thickened.

Serves four to six. Exquisite!

item.title: Marinade for Venison Steaks – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp dry white wine
  • 5 Tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tsp thyme
  • 1 tsp tarragon
  • 1 tsp chopped parsley
  • Salt & pepper to taste

Instructions

Mix all ingredients of the marinade together. Place steaks in a shallow dish and pour marinade over. Let stand for at least 2 hours, turning steaks once.

Remove steaks from the marinade and season with salt and pepper. Cook on a very hot grill to your liking.

item.title: Marshmallow Rolls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 30 lg or 120 mini colored marshmallows
  • 1-1/2 cup Eagle Brand milk
  • 2 cups graham cracker crumbs or vanilla wafers
  • 2/3 cup maraschino cherries (drained)
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2-1/2 cup coconut (fine cut)
  • Few grains salt

Instructions

Blend marshmallows, milk, crumbs or wafers, salt, vanilla and cherries. Butter your hands and form ingredients into rolls. Roll rolls in coconut. Place in wax paper. Refrigerate to cure for 24 hours. Freeze and slice off in just the right size rounds.

item.title: Melody Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 1/3 cup sugar
  • 2 Tbsp lemon juice
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1/3 cup ketchup
  • ½ cup Canola oil

Instructions

Measure all ingredients into a bottle or jar.

Cover tightly and shake well, chill thoroughly.

Shake well before serving.

Yield: 1-1/4 cups.

item.title: Mexican Chicken and Rice (cw)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
  • 1 medium green pepper, chopped
  • 1 small onion, chopped
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 8-3/4 oz tin kernel corn, drained
  • 1 cup chicken broth
  • 1 cup salsa
  • ½ to 1 tsp salt
  • ½ to 1 tsp chili powder, optional
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1-1/2 cups uncooked instant rice
  • ½ to 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese

Instructions

In large skillet, sauté the chicken, green pepper and onion in oil until chicken is no longer pink and vegetables are crisp-tender. Add the corn, broth, salsa, salt, chili powder if desired and pepper. Bring to a boil.

item.title: Microwave Carrot Casserole (cw)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 lbs carrots, shredded
  • ¼ cup water
  • 1 10-3/4 oz tin condensed cream of celery soup (undiluted)
  • 1 cup (4 oz) shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • ¼ cup milk
  • ½ tsp ground mustard
  • 2 tbsp dried bread crumbs
  • 1 tbsp melted butter or margarine

Instructions

In a 1-1/2 qt microwave-safe dish, combine carrots and water. Cover and microwave on high for 6 – 8 minutes or until crisp-tender, stirring once; drain and return to dish.

item.title: Mock Maple Syrup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups light brown sugar
  • 1 cup boiling water
  • ½ tsp maple flavoring

Instructions

Combine sugar and water, bring to boiling point and simmer 5 minutes. Remove from heat, add flavouring and stir well. Store in covered jar.

Makes about 2-1/2 cups.

item.title: Mom’s Mock Maple Syrup – Aboriginal Cooking
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 cups water
  • 1 tsp margarine or butter
  • ½ tsp maple extract

Instructions

Combine brown sugar, white sugar and water. Bring to a boil and simmer over medium heat – stirring constantly – for half an hour. Stir in margarine or butter and maple extract. Ready to serve over your pancakes.

item.title: Momma’s Pickled Beets
item.description:
Ingredients

  • About 5 lbs beets
  • 1-1/2 tbsp pickling spices
  • 2 cups white vinegar
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 cups water
  • 1-1/2 tbsp coarse salt

Instructions

Prepare beets by boiling till tender. Mix and bring to a boil the vinegar, sugar, salt, water and spices (tied in a bag if used). Boil for 5 minutes and keep hot.

Peel beets and, if they are large, cut them into bite-size chunks into hot sterilized jars. Pour hot brine solution on top, filling to ½ inch from top. Wipe rim of jars and put on prepared lids. Tighten screw top just fingertip tight.

item.title: Moose Stew - (metis Cookhouse by Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 lbs moose, cut in stewing pieces
  • 2 carrots
  • ¼ lb butter or margarine
  • 2 parsnips
  • 6 cups water
  • 1 small turnip
  • Salt & Pepper to taste
  • 10 medium potatoes

Instructions

Brown moose meat in hot butter or margarine. Add water, salt & pepper. Simmer 30 minutes then add chopped onions and cook on low for one hour.

But up and add 2 carrots, 2 parsnips, 1 small turnip and 10 medium potatoes and cook for 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.

item.title: Mustard Bean Pickles (purity Cook Book)
item.description:
Instructions

Wash and trim ends from 1 (6-quart) basket young yellow string beans. Cut into 1” lengths. This amount will measure about 16 cups prepared beans. Cook in boiling salted water until just tender. MUSTARD SAUCE: Mix together in a large sauepan or kettle 3 cups sugar, 1 Tbsp turmeric 2 tsp salt ½ cup dry mustard ½ cup all-purpose flour Blend in ½ cup vinegar and 1 cup water.

item.title: Nancy’s Special Fruit Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 250 gr. Cream cheese, softened
  • ½ cup sour cream
  • ¼ cup white sugar
  • ¼ cup packed brown sugar
  • 1 – 2 Tbsp real maple syrup

Instructions

Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Garnish with maple sugar leaves, if desired.

JUDY’S ESPECIALLY EASY FRUIT DIP

250-gr. Pkg. Cream cheese, softened container of marshmallow cream for sundaes (microwave for about 10 seconds to soften, if necessary.

Mix both ingredients thoroughly.

item.title: Nancy’s Special Fruit Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 250 gr. Cream cheese, softened
  • ½ cup sour cream
  • ¼ cup white sugar
  • ¼ cup packed brown sugar
  • 1 – 2 Tbsp real maple syrup

Instructions

Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Garnish with maple sugar leaves, if desired.

JUDY’S ESPECIALLY EASY FRUIT DIP

250-gr. Pkg. Cream cheese, softened container of marshmallow cream for sundaes (microwave for about 10 seconds to soften, if necessary.

Mix both ingredients thoroughly.

item.title: Nests with Coconut and Candies – Makes 24
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Prep. Time - 15 minutes
  • Microwave: - 2 minutes
  • 2 Tbsp butter or margarine
  • 3 cups miniature marshmallows
  • 4 cups Post Fruity, Cocoa or Cinna-Crunch Pebbles Cereal
  • Baker’s Angel Flake Coconut

Instructions
