Drain tofu; cut into ¼ “ pieces, place between layers of paper towel and weight down with a dinner plate; Let stand 10 minutes to compress and remove excess water. NOTE: I found this wasted too many paper towels; I placed tofu between two dinner plates, put a heavy weight on top and kept tipping the plates to pour off the water. It didn’t take long.). Heat oil, 2 Tbsp broth and ginger in a frypan. Add celery, onion and red pepper; stir-cook over medium heat 3 min.

item.title: Three-Bean Cassoulet
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cans (l4-1/2 oz each) stewed tomatoes
  • 1 can (19 oz) garbanzo beans or chickpeas, rinsed and drained
  • 1 can (15-1/2 oz) great northern beans, rinsed and drained
  • 1 can (15 oz) butter beans, rinsed and drained
  • 1 cup finely chopped carrots
  • 1 cup finely chopped onion
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 1 bay leaf
  • 2 tsp dried parsley flakes
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • ½ tsp dried thyme
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

In an ungreased 3-qt baking dish, combine all ingredients. Cover and bake at 350 for 60-70 minutes or until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally. Discard bay leaf before serving.

item.title: Tarragon Potato Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/3 lbs red potatoes, unpeeled
  • 1 small clove garlic
  • ¾ tsp dried tarragon
  • ½ cup no-oil Italian dressing
  • ½ tsp dried parsley flakes
  • 2 Tbsp minced green onions
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • ¾ tsp lemon juice

Instructions

Cook potatoes in boiling water to cover 15 minutes or until tender. Drain well, and cool. Cut into ¼” slices, and set aside. Combine tarragon, parsley, mustard and garlic in container of an electric blender. Cover and process 30 seconds or until pureed. Add Italian dressing and process until blended. With blender running, slowly add lemon juice, and continue to process dressing mixture until smooth. Add dressing mixture to cooked potato slices; add minced green onions, and toss well to coat. Cover and chill. Yield: 8(1/2 cup) servings.

item.title: Strawberry Raspberry Trifle
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups cold fat-free milk
  • 3 cups sliced fresh strawberries
  • 2 pkg (1 oz each) sugar-free instant white chocolate pudding mix
  • 1 prepared angel food cake (14 oz), cut into 1” cubes
  • 3 cups fresh raspberries
  • 3 whole strawberries, quartered
  • 1 carton (8 oz) reduced-fat frozen whipped topping, thawed

Instructions

In a bowl, whisk milk and pudding mix for 2 minutes or until slightly thickened. Place a third of the cake cubes in a trifle bowl or 3-1/2- qt serving bowl. Top with a third of the pudding, 1 cup sliced strawberries, 1-1/2 cups raspberries and a third of the whipped topping. Layer with a third of the cake and pudding, 1 cup strawberries and a third of the whipped topping. Top with remaining cake, pudding, strawberries, raspberries and whipped topping. Garnish with quartered strawberries. Serve immediately or refrigerate.

item.title: Sweet Pepper Chowder
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups chicken broth
  • 6 medium potatoes, peeled & shredded
  • 4 medium carrots, shredded
  • 4 celery ribs
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 1 large green pepper, diced
  • 1 large sweet red pepper, diced
  • 1 small sweet yellow pepper, diced
  • ½ cup all-purpose flour
  • 1-1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp Italian seasoning
  • ¼ to ½ tsp pepper
  • 1 cup water
  • 4 cups 2% milk

Instructions

In a Dutch oven or soup kettle, combine the broth, potatoes, carrots, celery and onion; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 20 minutes. Add the peppers; return to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until vegetables are tender. In a bowl, combine the flour, salt, Italian seasoning, Pepper and water until blended. Stir into the vegetable mixture. Bring to a boil, cook and stir for 2 minutes until thickened. Reduce heat. Stir in milk, heat through (do not boil).

item.title: Spinach Cups
item.description:
These vegetables-filled bread cups can be refrigerated up to 2 hours before baking without getting soggy.

Ingredients

  • 9 (1/2 oz) slices very thinly sliced whole wheat bread
  • ½ cup light process cream cheese, softened
  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • 1 (2-oz) jar diced pimento, drained
  • 1 (10-oz) pkg frozen chopped spinach, thawed
  • 1 small clove garlic, minced
  • 2 tsp reduced-calorie margarine
  • 1 tsp hot sauce
  • ½ cup finely chopped onion
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Trim crusts from bread; reserve crusts for another use. Flatten bread slices, using a rolling pin. Cut slices into quarters. Press into miniature (1-1/3”) muffin pans coated with cooking spray. Bake at 350 degrees for 5 to 7 minutes or until lightly browned. Drain spinach, press between paper towels to remove excess moisture. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; add margarine. Place over medium heat until margarine melts. Add onion, and sauté 4 to 5 minutes or until tender. Stir in spinach; cook 2 to 3 minutes or until thoroughly heated. Combine cream cheese and next 5 ingredients in a bowl; add spinach mixture, stirring well. Spoon into bread cups (2 tsp per cup). Bake at 350 degrees for 5 minutes or until thoroughly heated.

item.title: Spicy Chili Mac
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ lb firm tofu, drained and crumbled
  • 1-1/2 cup finely chopped onion
  • 1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes with puree, undrained
  • 1-1/3 cup finely chopped green pepper
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced 2 (15 oz) can red kidney beans, drained
  • 1 Tbsp chili powder
  • 7 oz wagon wheel pasta, uncooked
  • 1 tsp ground cumin
  • ¼ plus 3 Tbsp (1-3/4 oz) shredded reduced-fat Cheddar cheese
  • ½ tsp dried crushed red pepper

Instructions

Coat a Dutch oven with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add onion, green pepper, and garlic; sauté until tender. Add chili powder and next 2 ingredients; sauté 1 minute. Stir in tofu and tomatoes. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, 15 minutes. Add beans; cook 10 minutes or until thoroughly heated. Cook pasta according to package directions, omitting salt and fat. Drain. To serve, place pasta in individual bowls; top with bean mixture and cheese.

item.title: Spicy Beef Chili
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ tsp ground cumin
  • 1 lb ground round
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup chopped onion
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 2 cloves garlic, chopped
  • 1/8 tsp ground red pepper
  • 1 (16 oz) can red kidney beans, rinsed and drained
  • Dash hot sauce
  • ½ can canned no-salt- added beef broth,
  • 1 (14 oz) can no-salt- added whole undiluted
  • tomatoes, undrained and chopped
  • 2 tsp cornstarch
  • 1-1/2 cup no-salt- added tomato juice
  • 1/4 cup plus 2 Tbsp (1-1/2 oz) shredded 40% less-fat Cheddar cheese
  • 1 tsp ground coriander

Instructions

Coat a Dutch oven with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add ground round; cook until meat is browned, stirring to crumble. Remove meat from pat; drain and pat dry with paper towels. Wipe drippings from pan with a paper towel. Coat Dutch oven with cooking spray. Add onion and garlic; sauté 5 minutes or until onion is tender. Return meat to Dutch oven. Add beans and next 6 ingredients; bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Combine beef broth and cornstarch, stirring well; add to beef mixture. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly, and cook 1 minute or until thickened and bubbly. Ladle into individual bowls, sprinkle with cheese. Serve immediately.

item.title: Spaghetti With Savory Sauce
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 lb freshly ground raw turkey
  • 1-1/2 (6 oz) cans no-salt added tomato paste
  • 3 Tbsp minced onion
  • 6 oz spaghetti, uncooked
  • 2 cup plus 3 Tbsp water
  • 1 Tbsp freshly grated Parmesan cheese
  • 2 tsp dried Italian seasoning
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder

Instructions

Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add turley and onion; cook over medium heat until turkey is lightly browned, stirring to crumble. Drain and pat dry with paper towels. Wipe drippings from skillet with a paper towel. Add turkey mixture, water, and next 4 ingredients to skillet. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes or until thickened, stirring occasionally. Cook spaghetti according to package directions, omitting salt and fat; drain. Place on a serving platter; top with sauce. Sprinkle with cheese.

item.title: Shrimp Dippers
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 14 fresh snow pea pods, trimmed
  • 28 large cooked, peeled, and deveined shrimp

Instructions

Arrange pea pods in a vegetable steamer over boiling water. Cover and steam 1 minute or until crisp-tender. Remove pea pods and chill.

Separate pea pods lengthwise into 2 pieces. Wrap a pea pod half around each shrimp and secure with a pick.

Yield: 28 appetizers.

Diabetic Exchange: Free. Calories 7, Carbohydrate 0.1 g, Protein 1.4 g, Fat 0.1 g, Cholesterol 12 mg, Fiber 0 g, Sodium 14 mg.

item.title: Linguine With Fresh Tomatoes
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8 oz uncooked linguine
  • 2 medium tomatoes, chopped
  • 6 green onions, sliced
  • ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • ¼ cup minced fresh basil or 4 tsp dried basil
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 1 tsp salt (reduce this, if necessary)
  • ½ tsp pepper
  • 3 Tbsp non-hydrogenated margarine

Instructions

Cook pasta according to package directions, Meanwhile, in a large serving bowl, combine the tomatoes, onions, Parmesan cheese, basil, garlic, salt and pepper. Drain pasta and toss with margarine. Add to tomato mixture, toss to coat.

item.title: Lemon Turkey Cutlets
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 (2-oz) turkey breast cutlets
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2-1/2 Tbsp all-purpose flour
  • ¼ tsp freshly ground pepper
  • 2 tsp olive oil, divided
  • 2 Tbsp lemon juice

Instructions

Place cutlets between two sheets of heavy-duty plastic wrap; flatten to 1/8-inch thickness, using a meat mallet or rolling pin. Dredge in flour; set aside. Heat 1 tsp oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat until hot. Add half of cutlets, and cook 3 minutes on each side or until browned. Transfer cutlets to a serving platter; keep warm. Repeat procedure with remaining 1 tsp oil and cutlets. Sprinkle cutlets with salt, pepper, and lemon juice.

item.title: Fluffy Dumplings
item.description:
Flavor these dumplings with herbs like dill, caraway and basil for unique taste variations. These dumplings are not doughy at all, but become light and fluffy as they steam over simmering stew or soup.

Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 Tbsp margarine or butter
  • 1/3 cup 2% milk

Instructions

Combine flour, baking powder and salt. Cut in butter until mixture resembles fine crumbs. Add milk; stir until just moistened. Divide into 6 portions and spoon onto bubbling stew or soup. Cover tightly and simmer 15 minutes without lifting the lid.

item.title: Fat-Free Gravy
item.description:
Call this a waist-watcher gravy. There are only 16 calories in one serving. Serve it over meatloaf, roast meat and mashed potatoes.

Ingredients

  • 1 cup beef broth and defatted roast drippings combined
  • 2 tsp all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp cornstarch
  • 1 tsp ketchup
  • Pinch each of salt, freshly ground pepper and basil

Instructions

Combine beef broth, flour and cornstarch in a screw-top jar, cover and shake until well combined. Pour into saucepan, stir in ketchip, salt, pepper and basil. Stir-cook over medium heat about 2 minutes until gravy thickens.

item.title: Far East Crab Cakes
item.description:
If you’d like to serve this as an appetizer, divide the crab mixture evenly into 16 small patties, These smaller versions will have about 31 calories each.

Ingredients

  • 2 (6oz) cans lump crabmeat, drained
  • 1 tsp pepper
  • ½ cup finely chopped water chestnuts
  • 1 tsp dark sesame oil
  • ¼ cup soft breadcrumbs
  • 3 Tbsp reduced-calorie margarine
  • 1 Tbsp plus 1 tsp dried parsley flakes
  • Vegetable cooking spray

Instructions

Combine first 7 ingredients in a small bowl; stir well. Shape into 8 patties. Coat a large. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add crabmeat patties and cook 6 to 8 minutes or until lightly browned on both sides, turning once.

item.title: Dip'n Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 250 gr tub spreadable cream cheese
  • ½ to ¾ cup sour cream
  • 1 cup salsa
  • 1-1/2 cups grated Cheddar cheese
  • 1 cup cubed cooked chicken (use left-over chicken or grill up a chicken breast)
  • 1 bag bite-size Nacho chips

Instructions

Preheat oven of 350 degrees. Blend cream cheese with sour cream until mixture is spreadable. Spread mixture into a 6-cup oven-safe serving dish.

Spread salsa over mixture, using enough salsa to cover evenly. Arrange chicken, then the cheese evenly over salsa.

item.title: Dilled Garden Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 (16 oz) carton 1% low-fat cottage cheese
  • ½ tsp dried dillweed
  • 2 Tbsp tarragon vinegar
  • 1 Tbsp skim milk
  • 1 Tbsp finely chopped green onions
  • Dash of coarsely ground pepper
  • 1 Tbsp dried parsley flakes
  • Fresh parsley sprigs (optional)

Instructions

Place cottage cheese and vinegar in container of an electric blender or food processor; cover and process until smooth. Combine cottage cheese mixture, green onions, and next 4 ingredients in a medium bowl; stir well. Transfer cheese mixture to a small serving bowl; cover and chill. If desired, garnish with parsley sprigs and serve with Shrimp Dippers.

item.title: Crispy Orange Roughy (Fish)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 egg whites, lightly beaten
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 Tbsp water
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ¼ cup cornmeal
  • 6 (4 oz) orange roughy fillets
  • ¼ cup Parmesan cheese
  • 3 Tbsp all-purpose flour
  • ½ tsp dried oregano
  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ tsp dried parsley flakes
  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil

Instructions

Combine egg whites and water in a shallow dish. Combine cornmeal and next 5 ingredients in a medium bowl; stir well. Sprinkle each side of fillet with flour, Dip fillets into egg white mixture, and dredge in cornmeal mixture. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; add oil. Place skillet over medium-high heat until hot. Add fillets, and cook 4 to 5 minutes on each side or until golden and fish flakes easily and flakes easily when tested with a fork.

item.title: Crispy Oven-Fried Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup plus 2 Tbsp frozen egg substitute, thawed
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 Tbsp water
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1 cup crispy rice cereal, crushed
  • 1 (3-lb) broiler-fryer, cut up and skinned
  • 1/3 cup toasted wheat germ
  • ¼ all-purpose flour
  • 1 Tbsp instant minced onion Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ tsp salt-free herb and spice blend

Instructions

Combine egg substitute and water in a shallow dish. Combine cereal and next 6 ingredients in a shallow dish. Place chicken and flour in a large zip-top plastic bag; seal bag, and shake until chicken is well coated. Dip chicken in egg substitute mixture; dredge in cereal mixture. Place chicken on rach of a broiler pan coated with cooking spray. Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 1 hour or until chicken is tender.

item.title: Creamy French Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup ketchup
  • ½ cup reduced-fat mayonnaise
  • 3 Tbsp cider vinegar
  • 3 Tbsp honey
  • 2 Tbsp water
  • 1 Tbsp olive or canola oil
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • ½ tsp ground mustard
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • Salad greens & vegetables of your choice

Instructions

In a blender or food processor, combine the first nine ingredients; cover and process until blended. Serve over salad. Refrigerate leftovers. Yield: 1-3/4 cups

Nutritional Analysis: One serving (2 Tbsp dressing) equals 70 calories, 4 g fat (1 g saturated fat), 3 mg cholesterol, 318 mg sodium, 10 g carbohydrate, trace fiber, trace protein.

item.title: Creamy Coleslaw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup light sour cream
  • 1/3 cup light mayonnaise
  • ¼ cup 1% milk
  • ¼ cup Splenda Granular
  • 2 Tbsp vinegar
  • 1 Tbsp lemon juice
  • 1 tsp bottled horseradish (Optional)
  • Generous ¼ tsp salt (Optional)

Instructions

Per ¾ cup Serving of Coleslaw: Calories 80, Fat 4.5 gr (.5 saturated), Carbohydrate 8 gr, Fiber 2 gr, Protein 1 gr, Sodium 210 ml

Diabetic exchange = 1 Vegetable, 1 fat

WW = 2 points

item.title: Creamed Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 medium round red potatoes, peeled and sliced
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 small onion, peeled and sliced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 4 green onions, coarsely chopped
  • 1 cup skim milk
  • 2 (20 oz) cans no-salt added chicken broth, undiluted
  • 1/8 tsp ground white pepper
  • 1/8 tsp ground nutmeg

Instructions

Combine first 5 ingredients in a 3-quart saucepan; cover and simmer 20 minutes or until potato is tender. Transfer potato mixture in batches to container of an electric blender or food processor; cover and process until smooth. Combine pureed mixture with milk and remaining ingredients. Serve warm or chilled Yield: 9 (3/4 cup) servings.

item.title: Chinese-Style Whole Pickerel
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 green onions
  • 2 Tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 carrots
  • 2 tsp Brown Sugar Twin
  • 2 Tbsp sesame oil
  • 1 Tbsp dry sherry
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 3 lb whole pickerel, gutted & cleaned
  • 1 tsp finely grated ginger root (I (Mom) used ginger powder – about ¼ tsp)

Instructions

To prepare sauce, cut the green onions and carrots into 2-inch pieces. Then thinly slice these pieces into very thin julienne strips, set aside. Heat oil in a small frying pan. Add garlic and ginger and sauté for a minute or two. Add the remaining ingredients, except fish and stir until sugar is dissolved and mixture is heated through. Remove from heat. Stir in vegetables.

item.title: Chicken Vegetable Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cup water
  • 1 cup sliced carrot
  • 1 Tbsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules
  • 1 (8-oz)can mushroom stems and pieces,
  • 1 (14-1/2 oz) can no-salt added whole drained tomatoes, undrained and chopped
  • 1 cup diced zucchini
  • ¼ cup dried minced onion
  • 1 cup cooked chicken breast (skinned before cooking and cooked without salt or fat)
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • ¾ tsp minced garlic
  • 2 Tbsp dry red wine

Instructions

Combine first 7 ingredients in a Dutch oven. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes. Add carrot; cover and simmer 10 minutes. Add mushrooms and remaining ingredients; simmer, uncovered, 8 minutes. Yield: 7 (1 cup) servings.

item.title: California Coleslaw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shredded cabbage
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 cup shredded red cabbage
  • ¼ cup commercial no-oil Italian dressing
  • ½ cup shredded carrot
  • ¼ cup Splenda or Brown Sugar Twin
  • ½ cup chopped green pepper
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • ½ cup chopped sweet red pepper
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • ¼ cup chopped onion
  • 1-1/2 tsp minced fresh parsley

Instructions

Combine first 7 ingredients in a large bowl, stirring well. Set aside. Combine vinegar and remaining ingredients in a jar; cover tightly, and shake vigorously. Pour dressing mixture over vegetables, tossing gently to coat. Cover and chill. To serve, stir coleslaw lightly, and serve with a slotted spoon. Yield: 8 (1/2) cup servings.

item.title: Broccoli-Cauliflower Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups water
  • ¼ tsp ground mace
  • 1 (10 oz) pkg frozen chopped broccoli, thawed
  • 1 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 (10 oz) pkg frozen cauliflower, thawed
  • 3 cups skim milk, divided
  • 1/3 cup chopped onion
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp chicken-flavored bouillon granules
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Combine first 5 ingredients in a Dutch oven; cover and bring to a boil. Cook 5 minutes or until (do not drain); stir in mace. Transfer in batches to container of an electric blender; cover and process until smooth. Return to Dutch oven. Dissolve cornstarch in ½ cup milk; stir into vegetable mixture. Add remaining 2-1/2 cups milk, salt and pepper; cook over medium heat until thickened and bubbly, stirring frequently. Yield: 15 (1/2 cup) servings.

item.title: Barley Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 celery ribs, chopped
  • 2 medium carrots, finely chopped
  • 7 green onions, chopped
  • 4 garlic cloves, minced
  • 1 Tbsp non-hydrogenated margarine
  • 1-1/2 cups uncooked medium pearl barley
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 4-1/2 cups chicken broth, divided
  • 2 Tbsp minced fresh parsley
  • ½ cup slivered almonds

Instructions

In a large ovenproof Skillet, sauté the celery, carrots, onions and garlic in margarine until tender. Stir in the barley, salt and pa=epper. Stir in 2-1/2 cups broth. Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.

item.title: Applesauce Currant Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup vegetable oil
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ cup granulated sugar substitute
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup quick-cooking oats, uncooked
  • ½ cup unsweetened applesauce
  • ½ cup currants
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 tsp ground allspice
  • ½ cup whole wheat flour
  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ cup all-purpose flour

Instructions

Combine oil and sugar substitute in a bowl. Add egg; beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until fluffy. Stir in applesauce and vanilla. Combine whole wheat flour and next 6 ingredients; add to oil mixture, stirring well. Cover; chill 15 minutes. Drop by heaping teaspoon onto baking sheets coated with cooking spray. Bake at 375 degrees for 10 minutes.

item.title: Applesauce Oatmeal Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup whole wheat flour
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp nutmeg
  • ¾ cup rolled oats
  • ¼ cup Brown Sugar Twin
  • ½ cup raisins or ½ cup carob chips(optional)
  • 1 egg ¼ cup oil
  • 1/3 cup milk
  • 2/3 cup applesauce

Instructions

Mix dry ingredients well with a fork.

Beat egg, then add oil and milk.

Stir in applesauce.

Stir this into the dry ingredients, mixing only until moistened.

item.title: Applesauce Bran Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup All-Bran (Cereal)
  • ¼ cup milk
  • 1 cup applesauce
  • 1/3 cup oil
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup raisins or ½ cup carob chips(optional)
  • ¾ cup all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup whole wheat flour
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/3 cup Brown Sugar Twin

Instructions

Stir All-Bran, milk, applesauce, oil and egg together.

Stir together the dry ingredients.

Add bran mixture to dry ingredients, stirring just to moisten.

item.title: Lisa's Light Dessert
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 pkg cherry Jello
  • 2 cups boiling water
  • 1 14-oz can of crushed pineapple
  • 1 can cherry pie filling

Instructions

Mix and let set. Top with Light Cool Whip.

item.title: Maple Apple Cobbler
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 cup apple juice
  • ¼ cup Ontario Maple Syrup
  • 1 Tbsp lemon juice
  • 1-1/2 tsp cinnamon
  • 6 Ontario apples, unpeeled, cored, sliced
  • 1/3 cup raisins
  • ¼ cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • 1-1/4 cup biscuit mix
  • 1 tsp sugar

Instructions

Combine cornstarch, apple juice, maple syrup, lemon juice and 1 tsp of cinnamon. Place apples and raisins in a 2-1/2 quart shallow baking dish. Pour juice mixture over top. Bake at 400 F. (200 C.) for 20 minutes.

item.title: Grand-Peres
item.description:
A Favorite dessert in Quebec.

Ingredients

  • 2 Cups all-purpose flour
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp each salt & ground nutmeg (I (Mom) would cut this down to ½ tsp each)
  • 2 Tbsp white sugar
  • 2 Tbsp cooking oil
  • ¼ cup whole milk
  • 1 egg
  • 2 cups maple syrup (recipe for Mock Maple Syrup on Miscellaneous page)

Instructions

Sift flour with baking powder, salt, nutmeg and sugar. Stirring constantly to avoid lumps,gently add oil, then milk and, finally, egg.

item.title: Date Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Cups of Flour
  • 1 Cup of Brown Sugar
  • 1 1/4 Cups of Shortening
  • 1/2 Tsp Salt
  • 1 1/2 Lbs of Dates
  • 2 Cups of Rolled Oats
  • 1/2 Cup of Coconut
  • 1/2 Tsp Baking Soda
  • 1 Tsp Baking Powder

Instructions

  1. Rub first 8 ingredients until crumbly.
  2. Put 2/3 of  the mixture into the bottom of a 9x12 cake pan.
  3. Cook dates with 1 1/2 cups of water until soft.
  4. Add 1/4 - 1/2 cup of white sugar and 2 Tbsp of butter.  Mix well.
  5. Spread date mixture over crumbs in bottom of pain.
  6. Top with remaining crumb mixture.
  7. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.

item.title: Caramel Dumplings
item.description:
DUMPLINGS:

  • 1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ cup milk
  • 2 tbsp butter or margarine – softened
  • 2 tsp vanilla extract
  • ½ cup coarsely chopped peeled apple, optional

SAUCE:

Ingredients

  • 4 Tbsp butter or margarine
  • 1-1/2 cups packed brown sugar
  • 1-1/2 cups water

Instructions

In a skillet, heat the unsoftened butter, brown sugar and water to boiling.

Meanwhile, mix together all dumpling ingredients. Drop by tablespoonfuls into the simmering sauce. Cover tightly and simmer 20 minutes. Do not lift lid. Serve warm with cream or ice cream, if desired.

item.title: Apple Crisp
item.description:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.

Mix together:

Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 cup rolled oats
  • 1 cup lightly-packed brown sugar
  • 1 teaspoon cinnamon

Instructions

Cut in ½ cup butter or margarine. Set aside.

Wash, peel and slice apples to make 4 cups.

Mix about ¼ of the crumb mixture with the apples and spread in a buttered 8” x 12” baking dish. Cover with remaining crumbs and pat down slightly.

item.title: Amazingly Easy Apple Crisp
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 to 8 baking apples, such as McIntosh
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup coarsely chopped dried apricots or raisins (optional)
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2/3 cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup brown or granulated sugar
  • ½ cup healthy margarine
  • 3 Tbsp all-purpose flour
  • 2/3 cup blanched slivered almonds (optional)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Peel, core and slice apples. Place in a large bowl with apricots or raisins, if using. Stir ½ cup sugar with 3 Tbsp flour and cinnamon. Sprinkle over apples and toss to coat evenly.

item.title: Yummy Oatmeal Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1-1/2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • ½ cup milk
  • ½ tsp white vinegar (I (Mom) didn’t use this)
  • 1-3/4 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 3 cups quick-cooking rolled oats
  • ½ cup raisins (optional)
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts or sliced almonds (optional)

Instructions

Cream together butter and brown sugar. Add the vinegar to the milk and set it aside for one minute. Add eggs to the butter and sugar mixture one at a time, mixing until light and fluffy. Add the milk with vinegar and mix until well incorporated.

item.title: Whipped Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb butter
  • ½ lb margarine
  • ½ cup cornstarch
  • 1 cup sifted icing sugar
  • 3 cups sifted flour

Instructions

Cream butter and margarine gradually with cornstarch and sugar. Add flour, a little bit at a time, and whip mixture until it looks like cream. The more they are beat – the better they are.

Drop by spoonfuls on cookie sheet and bake in a 325 degree oven for 22 minutes.

item.title: Thimble Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 1 cup sifted flour
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • Nuts, chopped finely or corn flakes crushed
  • 1 egg, separated
  • Jelly
  • 1 tsp flavouring

Instructions

Cream butter. Add sugar gradually and mix well. Add well Beaten egg yolk, flavouring and flour. Shape into balls about the size of a walnut. Dip in unbeaten egg white, then in finely chopped nuts, almonds, walnuts or soya bean flakes or corn flakes. Dent in center with a thimble and bake in a moderate oven at 350 degrees for 5 minutes and dent again. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes more. Fill with jelly or jam while still hot if planning not to freeze them.

item.title: The Ultimate Chocolate Chip Cookie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3/4 cup Golden Crisco
  • 1-1/4 cup lightly packed brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 Tbsp milk
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 3/4 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup chocolate chips
  • 1 cup coarsely chopped pecans or walnuts

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees.

Cream Golden Crisco and brown sugar in large bowl until light. Add egg, milk and vanilla, beating 1 minute or until well blended.

item.title: Star Sugar Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 2-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2 tsp cream of tartar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • Raisins
  • Fine sugar

Instructions

Heat oven to 375 degrees. Blend shortening and sugar. Beat in eggs and vanilla. Blend in sifted dry ingredients. On lightly floured board, pat or roll dough to ¼-inch thickness. Cut in 3-inch rounds. Arrange on greased cookie sheet. Press 3 raisins in center of each cookie.

item.title: Rolled Cut-Out Sugar Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups flour
  • 1 cup butter or shortening
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla or lemon extract
  • 1 tbsp milk

Instructions

Cream butter, sugar, eggs and milk. Add vanilla. Stir in sifted dry ingredients and blend well.

Roll out dough ¼” thick and cut in desired shapes. Bake at 375 degrees until done, about 8 – 10 minutes.

item.title: Peanut Butter Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 tbsp vanilla
  • 1 cup peanut butter
  • 3 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Cream shortening, sugar and eggs. Add peanut butter and vanilla. Add flour, salt and baking soda. Form into small balls and press down with a fork dipped into milk. Bake at 350 degrees for 12 to 15 minutes.

item.title: Oatmeal Cookies
item.description:
• 1 cup margarine or butter
  • 1-1/2 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg

Mix above ingredients well, then add:

Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1-1/4 cup oatmeal

Instructions

Mix well and bake in 350 degree oven ‘til golden brown.

This recipe came from Florence Burton, who had gotten it from Roland Lejambe.

item.title: Oatmeal Snack Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup butter or margarine, softened
  • 2/3 cup packed brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-1/2 old-fashioned rolled oats
  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 6-oz pkg sweetened dried cranberries, option
  • 2/3 cup white or semi-sweet chocolate chips, optional

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 F. Using electric mixer, beat better and sugar together in a medium mixing bowl until light and fluffy. Add eggs and mix well.

item.title: Never Fail Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup Canadian Corn Starch
  • ½ cup icing sugar
  • 1 cup sifted all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup butter (do not substitute)

Instructions

Sift cornstarch, icing sugar and flour together. Blend in butter with a spoon, mixing until a soft, smooth dough forms. Shape dough into 1” balls. Place about 1-1/2” apart on ungreased cookie sheet. Flatten with a lightly floured fork.

Bake in 300 F. (slow oven) for 20 – 25 minutes or until edges are slightly browned.

item.title: English Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb butter
  • 1 cup fruit sugar
  • 4 cups flour

Instructions

Cream butter. Add sugar. Mix well. Add flour a little at a time, mixing well each time. Roll out ½ inch thick. Cut with cookie cutter. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 325 degree oven.

Bake 15 minutes. Turn cookie sheet. Continue baking for another 15 minutes.

Credit to Lorry Bernard.

item.title: Easy Macaroons
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 8-oz pkgs shredded coconut
  • 1 15-oz (1-1/3 cup) Eagle Brand
  • 2 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Mix ingredients. Drop from teaspoon onto well-greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes. Cool slightly. Remove to rack. Makes about four dozen.

item.title: Easiest Chocolate Chip Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup light or dark brown sugar, packed
  • 1 large egg, slightly beaten
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together till well mixed. Drop by teaspoonful on a greased cookie sheet 2” apart.

Bake in a 350 degree oven till puffed and golden brown – about 10 minutes.

item.title: Dad's Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups raisins
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 cup lard or shortening
  • 2 cups sugar
  • 3 eggs, beaten
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 3-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Cook raisins in water for five minutes. Cool. Cream lard or shortening and sugar. Add eggs, raisins and vanilla. Add sifted dry ingredients. Drop by spoonfuls about 1-1/2” apart on greased cookie sheet. Bake in 350 degree oven for 12 to 15 minutes.Oven grate on top – pan of water under.Remove cookies from pan immediately.

item.title: Cream Puffs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 cup water
  • 4 eggs, unbeaten

Instructions

Heat butter and water. When butter is melted, add flour all at once and stir vigorously until mixture no longer sticks to sides of pan. Remove from stove, cool slightly; add eggs one at a time, beating after each egg is added. Drop by heaping tablespoons onto a well-greased baking sheet. Place 2 inches apart and bake in hot oven, 450 degrees F. for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 325 degrees F., and bake about 20 minutes longer. Remove from baking sheet and cool.

item.title: Colette's Skeanut Butter Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup peanut butter (plain or chunky)
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Mix egg and sugar together. Add peanut butter. Spoon onto greased cookie sheet. Press with a fork. Use small teaspoon and note that they really flatten and spread.

Bake at 350 F. for l0 minutes.

item.title: Coconut Macaroons
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 pkg (200 gr ea) flaked coconut
  • 1 can (300 ml) Eagle Brand Milk
  • 2 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1-1/2 tsp almond extract

Instructions

  1. Combine coconut, Eagle Brand, vanilla and almond extracts; mix well.

  2. Drop by rounded spoonfuls onto well-greased cookie sheets.

  3. Bake 12 at a time on middle rack of preheated 325 F. oven, 10 – 12 minutes or until browned around the edges. Remove immediately to wire racks, cool completely.

item.title: Chocolate Coconut Macaroon Logs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup sweetened condensed milk
  • 1 Tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 3 cups flaked coconut
  • 2 egg whites
  • 8 oz. Semi-sweet chocolate, melted (optional)

Instructions

In large bowl, blend together condensed milk, cocoa and vanilla; stir in flaked coconut until well mixed. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form; fold into coconut mixture.

Drop by tablespoonfuls onto lightly greased or parchment paper-lined baking sheet; shape into logs about 3” long. Bake at 300 degrees F for 30 minutes or until golden brown and firm. Let cool on wire racks. Dip one end of each log into chocolate (if using). Let stand on waxed paper until chocolate is set.

item.title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 eggs
  • ¼ cup white sugar
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup margarine or butter
  • 2 cups chocolate chips
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 pkg vanilla or chocolate instant pudding
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2-1/4 cups flour
  • Walnuts (optional)

Instructions

Mix egg, butter, sugars, vanilla.

Add instant pudding and the rest of the dry ingredients.

Bake for 10 minutes at 375 degree F. oven.

item.title: Butterscotch Shortbread Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb butter
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 2 cups flour

Instructions

Mix the ingredients together; roll out ½”thick.

Cut out with metal cookie cutters.

Bake 325 F. or 20 minutes on an ungreased cookie sheet.

NOTE FROM COLETTE PEPLINSKIE: I use a pastry cutter, then knead into a ball

item.title: Cherry Surprises
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • ½ cup coconut
  • 1-1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 tsp milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla Pinch of salt
  • Cherries

Instructions

Cream butter, mix all ingredients and make into balls around cherries. Roll in graham wafers.

NOTE: If using maraschino cherries, drain them well and dry them or icing will stick to them.

item.title: Best Peanut Butter Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup soft butter
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup peanut butter (Smooth or chunky)
  • 2-1/2 cups flour
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Use a wooden spoon or electric mixer to cream the butter in a large bowl. Add peanut butter and mix until well blended. Add white and brown sugars, a little at a time, mixing until they are blended in. Break an egg into a small bowl and stir. Add to the peanut butter mixture and mix well. Do the same with second egg. Sift flour, baking powder and salt over mixture; stir until well blended. Form into 1 to 1 inch balls. Place on greased cookie sheet. Flatten with a fork. Bake for 10 minutes. Makes 36 large cookies.

item.title: Beautiful Sugar Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup softened butter
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 Medium or Large egg (not Extra large or Jumbo)
  • 1 Tbsp milk or cream
  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg or cinnamon

Instructions

Thoroughly cream the butter by hand. Add sugar and mix until fluffy. Beat in the egg and then the milk.

In a separate bowl mix together all dry ingredients. Stir them into the butter. Knead the dough for just a minute. Chill the dough, then form the cookies as directed in the “General Instructions” and bake at 350 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes, or until the edges brown.

item.title: Beautiful Ginger Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup softened butter
  • ½ cup packed dark brown sugar
  • ¼ cup molasses
  • 1 medium or large egg (do not use extra large or jumbo)
  • 2-1/4 cups flour
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • 2 dashes cloves
  • 1 dash cayenne pepper

Instructions

Thoroughly cream the butter by hand. Add brown sugar and molasses, then the egg. In a separate bowl mix together all dry ingredients. Stir them into the butter mixture. Knead the dough briefly. Chill the dough, then form the cookies as directed in the “General Instructions” and bake at 375 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes, or until the edges brown.

item.title: Streamlined Honey Garlic Drumsticks
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup honey
  • 3 Tbsp soy sauce
  • 2 cloves garlic
  • 1 tsp ground ginger or 1” fresh ginger root
  • 1 tsp (any kind) vinegar
  • ¼ cup water
  • 2 lb drumsticks (about 12)

Instructions

Put the honey, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, vinegar and water into the container or a blender and whirl it around till smooth. Arrange drumsticks in 9” x 13” pan and pour sauce on top.

Bake at 350 F. for about an hour, basting from time to time with the sauce and turning the legs over about halfway through.

item.title: Russian Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Prep: 10 min. Total Time: 55 min.

Ingredients

  • 2 tsp vegetable oil
  • 6 small boneless skinless chicken breast halves (about 1-1/2 lbs.)
  • ½ cup Kraft Russian Dressing
  • ½ cup Kraft Pure Apricot jam
  • ½ pkg dry onion soup mix (about 3 Tbsp.)

Instructions

PREHEAT oven to 350 F. Heat oil in large skillet on medium-high heat. Add Chicken; cook 4 min. on each side or until browned. Remove chicken from skillet; place in 3-L baking dish.

item.title: Oven Barbecued Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

Preparation: 5 minutes Cooking: 30 minutes

  • 1 can condensed tomato soup
  • 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ tsp hot pepper sauce (optional)
  • 2 Tbsp packed brown sugar
  • 4 chicken breast halves (about 2 lbs)

Instructions

  1. In a small bowl, combine soup, vinegar, oil, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder and hot sauce.

  2. Remove skin from chicken. On rack in broiler pan, arrange chicken bone side up. Brush chicken with sauce. Broil 6” from heat for 25 to 30 minutes or until no longer pink, turning once and brushing often with sauce during cooking. Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice if desired.

item.title: One Pan Cacciatore Dinner
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb chicken pieces
  • Flour (for coating)
  • A few Tbsp olive oil
  • Handful of sliced peppers
  • Handful of sliced mushrooms
  • 1 can (19 oz) Aylmer Accents Italian Stewed Tomatoes
  • 2 cups cooked spaghetti noodles
  • Parmesan cheese for topping

Instructions

Coat chicken pieces in flour and brown in a few tablespoons of olive oil.

Add sliced peppers and mushrooms; cook until tender, stirring occasionally.

Pour in one can of Italian Stewed Tomatoes, cover and simmer for 30 minutes or until done.

item.title: Glazed Chicken With Julienne Veggies
item.description:
Ingredients

Preparation Time: 10 minutes - Cook Time: 25 minutes

  • 2 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tin condensed chicken broth
  • 4 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves
  • ¼ cup apple juice
  • 1 large carrot cut into thin strips
  • 1 Tbsp honey
  • 1 medium green pepper, cut in thin strips
  • 2 tsp Dijonaise mustard

Instructions

  1. In small bow, stir together cornstarch, broth, apple juice, honey and mustard – set aside.

item.title: Honey Hoisin Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 Tbsp hoisin sauce
  • 2 Tbsp liquid honey
  • 2 Tbsp Ketchup
  • 2 minced garlic cloves
  • ½ tsp sesame oil
  • 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts

Instructions

In small saucepan, combine soy sauce, hoisin, honey, ketchup and garlic. Cook over medium heat for 2 minutes, stirring often. Remove from heat: stir in sesame oil. Remove ¼ of the sauce for basting; set remaining sauce aside.

Place chicken on greased grill over medium heat; close lid and cook for 2 minutes. Turn and brush with sauce, cook for 3 to 4 minutes on each side or until no longer pink inside. Serve with reserved sauce. Makes 2 servings.

item.title: Chili Flappers
item.description:
Preparation: 10 minutes Baking time: 30 minutes

Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 lb chicken wings
  • ½ cup dry bread crumbs
  • ¼ cup flour
  • 1 Old El Paso Chili Seasoning Mix (35gr pkg)

Instructions

Heat oven to 350 degrees. Line a baking sheet with foil, grease lightly. With a sharp knife, remove wing tips, discard. Cut through wings at joint to yield two pieces each. Place bread crumbs, flour and chili seasoning mix in large plastic bag, shake to combine. Shake chicken wings in mix to coat thoroughly.

item.title: Spanish Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup rice
  • 4 tbsp butter
  • 3 – 4 onions, chopped
  • 1 28-oz can tomatoes
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1 red or green pepper, chopped
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 2 tsp salt

Instructions

Saute onions, garlic and pepper in butter. Add remaining ingredients and cook slowly on top of stove for 1 hour.

YIELD: Four servings.

item.title: Sausage and Pepper Stew
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp olive oil
  • 1 lb hot Italian sausage
  • 1 medium onion, thinly sliced
  • 2 lg green peppers, cored, seeded and cut into thin strips
  • 1 680-ml Hunt’s Italian tomato sauce
  • 1 cup water
  • 2/3 cup uncooked small pasta
  • 5 sprigs each: mint & basil tied together
  • Salt and ground black pepper to taste

Instructions

In a large skillet, heat oil; add sausage and onion. Cook until sausage is brown on all sides and onion is tender.

item.title: Shipwreck
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 large onions
  • 2 medium potatoes
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • ½ cup long grain rice
  • 1 cup chopped celery
  • salt and pepper
  • 1 tin tomato soup
  • 1 soup can boiling water

Instructions

Peel onions and slice over bottom of 2-quart casserole. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Slice peeled potatoes over onions. Salt and pepper potatoes. Pat ground beef over. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Sprinkle rice on next, followed by celery. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.

item.title: Ruth's Beef and Rice Supreme
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb hamburg
  • 1 cup rice
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1 tin tomato soup
  • 1 tin water
  • 3 onions

Instructions

Mix together uncooked hamburg, rice, salt, pepper, soup and water. Slice onions on top. Bake in a covered casserole in a 350 degree oven for 2 hours.

item.title: Rob's Baked Beans
item.description:
Empty 5-lb. bag of beans into pot.

Cover with water.

Boil for 1 hr. after water begins to bubble.

Drain beans and save the water.

Put 1/2 the beans in a dish and ½ in a pot.

To the beans in the pot, add:

Ingredients

  • ½ lb. shortening ( break in chunks).
  • ½ cup syrup.
  • a good handful of brown sugar.
  • 1 lb. of bacon, cut in small pieces.

Instructions

Put the other ½ of beans on top and mix with a large spoon.

item.title: Oven Fried Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups uncooked long grain rice
  • 1 tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 10-oz cans consommé soup
  • ½ cup oil
  • 2 10-oz cans mushrooms & liquid
  • Celery, onions and green pepper – to taste, chopped fine
  • 1 envelope onion soup mix

Instructions

Mix all ingredients in small roaster and bake in 350 degree oven for 1-1/2 hours.

item.title: Noodles Al Forno Napolitana
item.description:
Get supper on the table in a flash using only six ingredients. Toss a salad, warm up an Italian bread stick and you have dinner fit for royalty in about 30 minutes.

Ingredients

  • 1 lb broad egg noodles
  • 1 19-oz can tomato sauce
  • 4 cups ricotta cheese
  • ¼ cup sliced green onion
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • 1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions

Butter a 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Set aside. Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain well and return to pot. Stir tomato sauce, ricotta cheese, green onion and basil into pasta.

item.title: Mom's Macaroni & Cheese Casserole
item.description:
I’ve made this one so many times, I can recite it from memory. A favorite of Pep’s (and pretty much anyone else). So good that it convinced my girls that tomatoes aren’t the devil in stewed vegetable form.

Ingredients

  • 5 handfuls of elbow macaroni (Yep, handfuls. Not sure how, but my hand-size is the same as Mom’s and everyone else…but it still somehow works!)
  • 1 brick of medium cheddar cheese (I think they’re 400g bricks, but those long 2x4 sized ones)
  • Margarine
  • Salt/Pepper to taste
  • 1 Can of Stewed Whole Tomatoes
  • Water

Instructions

In a pot, boil enough water to boil 5 handfuls of macaroni. I throw a bit of salt and olive oil in there (for taste and so the noodles won’t stick).

item.title: Maritime Madness
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 tin mushroom soup
  • ½ cup chopped onions
  • 1 tbsp parsley flakes
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ½ tsp sage
  • 3 tbsp ketchup
  • 1 tbsp barbecue sauce
  • ½ tsp garlic powder
  • ½ cup rolled oats

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in large bowl. Mix well. Pat down in 1-1/2 qt casserole. Bake

covered in 350 degree F oven for 50 minutes. Remove cover and bake about 15 minutes to

item.title: Mom's Baked Beans
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs. navy beans
  • 1 onion, cut in two
  • ¼ lb. salt pork
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • ¼ cup catsup
  • 3 Tbsp. dry mustard
  • 4 Tbsp. molasses
  • Salt & Pepper to taste
  • ¼ cup shortening, in chunks

Instructions

This is sort of the recipe for baked beans that I (Mom) started with. They do not have to be boiled beforehand. This recipe can be altered to many ways – every cook puts his/her own touch to it.

item.title: Mabel's Easiest Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup raw long grain rice
  • 1-3/4 cup peeled and chopped tomatoes (or use one 14-oz can)
  • 1 small mild onion, chopped
  • 1 green pepper, chopped
  • 1-1/2 tsp salt – divided
  • 3 thick loin pork chops
  • 1 cup hot water (or 1 can consommé)
  • 2 tbsp chicken bouillon cordial – see below

Instructions

Measure raw rice into large shallow casserole. Stir tomatoes into it. (If time permits let stand 1

item.title: Lazy Perogy Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 15 lasagna noodles
  • 250 ml grated medium cheddar cheese
  • 500 ml cottage cheese
  • 500 ml mashed potatoes
  • 1 egg
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp onion salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 cup chopped onions

Instructions

*Add ½ - 1 lb crumbled cooked bacon to potato-cheese mixture

Cook noodles as directed on package. Drain. Line bottom of 9 x 13 inch pan.. In medium size

bowl, mix cottage cheese, egg and first amount of onion salt together. Spoon over noodles and

item.title: Lazy Man's Perogies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 15 medium-sized potatoes
  • 400 gr cheddar cheese
  • 1 small pkg Velveeta cheese
  • 1 lb bacon, diced
  • 3 large onions, diced
  • 1 pkg lasagna noodles
  • Sour cream
  • Shred 2 pkg of cheeses together and set aside.

Instructions

Dice bacon. Fry until almost cooked. Add diced onion and fry until golden brown. Set aside

to keep warm.

Peel and cook potatoes. When cooked, drain well. Add shredded cheeses while potatoes are

item.title: Husband's Delight Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb lean ground beef (Mom used 1-1/2 lb ground moose)
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 1 tsp salt (Mom omitted salt and it tasted just fine)
  • 1/4 tsp pepper
  • 2 (8-oz) tins tomato sauce
  • 4 cups dry egg noodles (1/2 lb)
  • 5 scallions, chopped (Mom used regular onions)
  • 1 pkg (3 oz) cream cheese, room temp
  • 1 (8-oz) container sour cream
  • 1/2 cup or more shredded cheddar cheese

Instructions

In a large skillet, cook meat over medium high heat, breaking up clumps with wooden spoon, until browned, 5 minutes. Carefully drain off drippings. Stir in garlic, sugar, salt, pepper and tomato sauce into meat. Cover, simmer 15 minutes.

item.title: Fluffy Potato Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Cups of mashed potatoes, hot or cold
  • 1 250g package of cream cheese, softened
  • 1 small onion, finely chopped
  • 3 Tbsp of flour
  • Salt & Pepper to taste

Instructions

Add everything together.  Beat until light and fluffy.  Spoon into greased casserole dish.  Bake at 300 degrees for 35 mins (maybe more).

item.title: Chuk's Chicken Fried Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 2 green peppers
  • 2 onions
  • 2 cups celery
  • mushroom pieces and stems
  • 1/2 cup soya sauce
  • 2 tins consommé soup
  • 4 cups minute rice

Instructions

Saute diced green peppers, chopped onions, chopped celery, mushroom in butter til onions are

transparent. Add soya sauce and soup. Bring this mixture to a boil and stir in minute rice.

Cover and cook on low heat for 1/2 hour.

Cooked chicken chunks, or ham, or egg can be added when rice is added.

item.title: Chili Beef Bake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb hamburg
  • 1 cup uncooked rice
  • 1-1/2 tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1 tsp chili powder
  • 2 minced onions
  • 1 28-oz tin canned tomatoes
  • 1-1/4 cup water
  • 1 tin mushroom (optional)

Instructions

Mix well and bake at 350 degrees F. for 2 hours, covered.

item.title: Chili Con Carne
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups chopped onion
  • 1/2 cup chopped green pepper
  • 2 lbs ground beef
  • 2 Tbsp fat
  • 1 (28-oz) tin red kidney beans
  • 1 (28-oz) tin tomatoes
  • 1–3 Tbsp chili powder
  • 1 Tbsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp pepper
  • 2 Tbsp vinegar

Instructions

Brown onion, green pepper and ground beef in 2 Tbsp fat.

Add kidney beans, tomatoes, chili powder, salt, pepper and vinegar.

Simmer one hour, stirring frequently.

Approx. 10 servings.

item.title: Cheesy Tuna Noodle Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups frozen vegetable blend with broccoli, carrots, cauliflower
  • 1 pkg Kraft Dinner Macaroni & Cheese
  • ¾ cup skim milk
  • ¼ cup Kraft Calorie-Wise Zesty Italian Dressing
  • 2 tins (170 gr each) Clover Leaf Chunk White Tuna, drained
  • 1 cup Kraft Double
  • Cheddar Shredded Cheese Light, divided

Instructions

PREHEAT oven to 375 F. Place vegetables in colander in sink. Cook Macaroni as directed on

package. Pour cooked Macaroni over frozen vegetables in colander to drain and quickly thaw

item.title: Casserole Cabbage Rolls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground chuck beef
  • 1 tbsp cooking oil
  • 1 chopped onion
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 3 tbsp raw long grain rice
  • 1 10-oz can tomato soup
  • 1 soup can of water
  • 3 cups coarsely chopped cabbage (packed)

Instructions

In large frying pan stir and break up the ground beef in oil for a minute or two then add onion,

salt, pepper and rice. Mix well while gently sautéing for 2 or 3 minutes, then add soup and

item.title: Campbell's Easy Macaroni and Cheese
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can Cheddar Cheese Soup
  • ½ soup can milk
  • dash ground black pepper
  • 4 cups hot cooked elbow macaroni, (about 2 cups uncooked)
  • 1 medium tomato, chopped
  • 1 green onion, sliced
  • 3 Tbsp dry bread crumbs
  • 2 Tbsp margarine or butter, melted

Instructions

Combine soup, milk and pepper in 1-1/2 quart baking dish.

Stir in macaroni, tomato and green onion. Mix bread crumbs with margarine and sprinkle over macaroni mixture.

item.title: Beef Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lb stewing beef
  • 1 pkg dry onion soup mix
  • 1 10-oz tin cream of mushroom soup
  • 1 cup red table wine

Instructions

Combine ingredients in casserole. Cook slowly for 2 – 3 hours in a 250 degree oven. Serve on a bed of egg noodles.

YIELD: Six to eight servings.

item.title: 7-Layer Dinner
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 potatoes, sliced thin
  • 1 onion, sliced thin
  • 3 carrots, sliced thin
  • 1/4 cup uncooked long grain rice
  • 1 cup frozen green peas
  • 12 sausages or wieners, sliced into l-inch pieces
  • 1 l0-oz tin tomato soup
  • 1 tin water

Instructions

Put everything in casserole in order given. If using sausages, boil for l0 minutes first. Mix

soup and water and pour over top. Cover and bake at 350 degrees F for 2 hours.

item.title: White Christmas Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter
  • 5 eggs
  • 1-1/4 cup sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 cup red glazed cherries
  • 1 cup green glazed cherries
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, one at a time. Add flour. Mix well. Add cherries and nuts. Mix well. Bake in greased angelfood tin or a pan lined with well-greased brown paper.

Bake for 1-1/2 – 2 hours at 300 degrees.

item.title: Tomato Soup Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ tsp nutmeg
  • ½ tsp cloves
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tin tomato soup
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 1 cup raisins or dates

Instructions

Cream together shortening and sugar. Fold in beaten egg. Add soup. Mix well. Stir in flour, spices, salt, baking soda, sifted together. Add raisins and nuts last. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Ice with cream cheese frosting if desired.

item.title: Super Moist Chocolate Mayo Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 box chocolate cake mix
  • 1 cup Hellmann’s Mayonnaise
  • 1 cup water
  • 3 eggs
  • 1 tsp ground cinnamon (optional)

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.

Blend all ingredients.

Bake as directed on the box.

item.title: Strawberry Jam Muffins
item.description:
Yield: 24 muffins Baking Time: 20-25 minutes

Preparation Time: 15 minutes Oven Temperature: 350 degrees

Ingredients

  • 1 cup sugar
  • ¾ cup margarine
  • 3 eggs
  • 1 tsp soda
  • 2 cups sifted flour
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1/3 cup sour milk
  • ¾ cup jam

Instructions

Cream sugar and margarine. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition.

Sift dry ingredients together. Add dry ingredients alternately with sour milk. Fold in ¾ cup jam. Fill muffin tins ¾ full (grease if not using liners).

item.title: Pineapple Cupcakes
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup shortening
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • ½ cup light brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-3/4 cup flour
  • ½ tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup crushed pineapple including some syrup
  • ½ cup water

Instructions

Into beater bowl measure shortening and sugars and beat until creamy. Beat in eggs. Remove beaters.

Sift together flour, baking powder and salt. Combine pineapple, water and vanilla. Stir flour mixture into creamed mixture alternately with the pineapple-water mixture until blended. Line the muffin pans with pleated paper cups and carefully spoon in batter to fill 7/8 full. Bake at 350 degrees 20 – 25 minutes. Cool in paper cups on cake rack.

item.title: Rhubarb Upside-Down Cake
item.description:
• 3 cups sliced fresh rhubarb, ½” pieces
  • 1 Tbsp unsalted butter, room temperature
  • ¼ cup strawberry or seedless raspberry jam
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1-1/2 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 2/3 cup butter, room temperature
  • 2 eggs
  • ¼ tsp each vanilla & almond extract
  • 1/3 cup milk

Put rhubarb in a medium bowl. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Generously coat a deep dish pie plate with margarine.

item.title: Nugget 1942 Johnny Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup yellow cornmeal
  • 1 cup stirred but unsifted cake flour
  • 5 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • 2 Tbsp corn or other cooking oil
  • 1/3 cup corn syrup
  • 1 Tbsp butter or drippings

Instructions

Turn the oven to 425 degrees. Next put the sifter over a bowl and into it measure the cornmeal, flour, baking powder and salt. Sift into bowl. At this stage measure the butter or drippings into a 9” x 9” pan and put it in the oven to heat up to sizzling point. To dry ingredients add egg, milk, oil and corn syrup and stir just enough to mix. Carefully remove hot pan from oven and pour batter into sizzling hot fat. Return to oven at 425 degrees and bake 20 minutes.

item.title: Nancy Lemon Coffee Cake
item.description:
• 1 large lemon or any flavour cake mix
  • 1 3-3/4 pkg Jello Lemon Instant pudding
  • 1 cup water
  • ½ cup oil
  • 4 eggs

Mix all together.

TOPPING;

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Grease tube pan. Sprinkle half topping mixture. Pour half of cake mixture. Add rest of topping mixture. Pour rest of batter on top.

Bake in a 350 degree oven for 50 minutes. (I use a large angel food cake tin.)

item.title: Mock Cheese Cake
item.description:
FILLING:

  • 1 14-oz fruit cocktail
  • 1 small carton sour cream
  • Sugar to taste 1 – 2 tsp.

Drain cocktail. Set out on paper towel to dry off. Mix with sour cream. Set aside.

MIX:

Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup graham wafer crumbs
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • ¼ cup melted butter

Instructions

Press in 8”x 8”or 6”x 11”pan. Bake until light brown. Cool. Pour filling on cool shell. Sprinkle remaining crumbs on top. Store in refrigerator several hours or overnight.

item.title: Mix-in-the-pan Carrot Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp nutmeg
  • ½ tsp each salt, baking soda, baking powder
  • 1-1/2 cups shredded carrots
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • 1/2 cup oil
  • 1/4 cup water
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla
  • icing sugar, optional

Instructions

In 8”or 9”square pan with fork mix well flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder and nutmeg. Add carrots, eggs, oil, water and vanilla.

Mix briskly with a fork until thoroughly blended. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven about 30 minutes or until pick inserted in center comes out clean and cake pulls away from sides of pan. Cool in pan on rack. Sprinkle with icing sugar or ice with cream cheese frosting.

item.title: Mexican Wedding Cake
item.description:
• 2 cups flour
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 2 eggs
  • 19-oz tin crushed pineapple

Mix and bake in a 9”x 9” greased pan in a preheated 350 degree oven for 50 – 60 minutes.

Ice with cream cheese frosting:

Ingredients

  • 8 oz cream cheese (250 gr.)
  • ¼ cup butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 lb icing sugar

item.title: Mirsh's Chocolate Zucchini Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ¾ tsp nutmeg
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup cocoa
  • 3 eggs
  • 2 cups sugar
  • ½ cup oil
  • ¾ cup buttermilk OR 2 Tbsp vinegar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 cups grated zucchini

Instructions

Mix all together. Bake in 350 degree F. oven for 40 – 60 minutes.

item.title: Hundred Dollar Cake
item.description:
First Step:

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 4 tbsp cocoa
  • 1-3/4 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • Pinch of salt

Second Step:

Ingredients

  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 1 cup cold water
  • 1 tsp vanilla (Beat well)

Instructions

Sift dry ingredients together. Add to liquid mixture and beat for 2 minutes. Bake in 9”x 9” cake pan for 40 minutes at 350 degrees. - 25 minutes for cupcakes.

item.title: Hot Milk Sponge Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 large eggs
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup milk
  • 3 tbsp margarine or butter
  • 1 cup all-purpose-flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder

Instructions

Into beater bowl break eggs and beat until fluffy then slowly add sugar until very light and doubled in bulk.  Beat in salt & vanilla.  In a pan heat together the milk and margarine OR butter to just below boiling point.  While very hot pour into egg mixture BEATING ALL THE TIME.  Remove beaters.

item.title: Gumdrop Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups pastry flour (1-3/4 cup all-purpose)
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 lb cooking gumdrops
  • 1 lb seedless raisins

Instructions

Cream sugar and butter; add eggs. In separate bowl, dredge gumdrops and raisins with ½ cup of the flour. To the creamed mixture, add 1 cup flour and ½ cup milk, beating well after each addition. Add remaining flour with baking powder and salt. Beat well. Add remaining milk and the floured raisins and gumdrops. Mix thoroughly. Bake in a well-greased loaf pan in a 350 degree oven for 1 hour.

item.title: German Fruit Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 1/3 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 3 tbsp milk
  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • Pinch of salt

Instructions

Cream butter and add sugar little by little, then add the eggs and salt. Sift flour with baking powder. Add to egg and sugar mixture alternately with milk. Beat well. Pour into a well-greased and floured flan pan. Bake 15 minutes in a 325 degree oven. Knock firmly on bottom of pan when you take it out of the oven to loosen it from pan. Cool. Invert on a place and fill it with fruit or filling of your choice. Top with whipped cream if desired.

item.title: Fruit Cocktail Cake
item.description:
300 degrees - 50 – 60 minutes

Ingredients for Cake:

  • 2 eggs
  • 1-3/4 cup W. Sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp Baking Soda
  • 1 – 14 oz tin fruit cocktail (drained but reserve juice) – Slightly crush fruit w/a fork

Ingredients for Topping:

Ingredients

  • 1-3/4 cup W. Sugar
  • ½ cup pure Carnation Milk
  • ½ cup butter or margarine

Instructions

Directions for Cake: Beat eggs with Sugar. Put juice & Baking Soda in eggs & beat. Add Fruit Cocktail & beat. Add flour & beat. Pour into a 13” x 15” well-greased pan. Bake for 50 - 60 minutes in a 300 degree oven. Test w/a toothpick for doneness.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Carrot Cake
item.description:
• 2 cups white sugar
  • 4 eggs
  • 1-1/2 cups oil
  • 2 Tbsp cinnamon
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 Tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 3 cups grated carrots
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Blend sugar and oil. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each one. Beat well. Sift together dry ingredients. Add gradually to first mixture. Add carrots and nuts last.

Bake in 9” x 15” pan 45 minutes approx. at 350 F.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Triple Fudge Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 package chocolate pudding mix (cooked type)
  • 1 package chocolate or devil’s food cake mix
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions

Prepare chocolate pudding mix according to package directions. Blend dry chocolate or devil’s food cake mix into hot pudding.

Pour into greased and floured 13" x 9" pan.

Sprinkle with chocolate chips. Bake at 350°F for 30–35 minutes.

item.title: Edna's Lemon Cake
item.description:
All who attended Creation Unique’s Camp Wanna-sew enjoyed Edna’s Lemon Cake.

  • 1 Lemon Cake Mix
  • 1 Jello Instant Pudding Mix

Add:

Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • 1 cup of water
  • ½ cup oil

Instructions

Mix well. Pour into pan and bake for 1 hour at 325 degrees.

For the glaze, Edna stirred lemon juice with lemon juice.

item.title: Double Strawberry Sheet Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg strawberry cake mix
  • 1 pkg strawberry Jello (4 servings)

Instructions

Dissolve Jello in ¾ cup boiling water. Add ½ cup cold water; set aside. Mix and bake cake as directed in a 9”x 13” pan. Cool 20 – 25 minutes. Poke deep holes about 1”apart in cake (still in pan) with a meat fork or large nail.

Pour Jello into holes.

Refrigerate cake while preparing Quick and Easy Icing made with whipped topping and instant pudding.

item.title: Corn Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup cornmeal
  • ½ cup sugar (use more than this)
  • ½ cup flour
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1-1/2 cup milk
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • ½ cup oil or shortening

Instructions

Heat oven to 425 F. Mix corn meal, sugar, flour, baking powder and salt. Add milk, eggs and oil stirring only to mix well. Pour into a 9” baking dish or casserole. Bake until light brown or about 25 minutes. Test till it comes out clean.

item.title: Carrot Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups sugar
  • 1 cup oil
  • 2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 4 eggs, beaten
  • 3 cups grated carrots
  • 1 cup finely chopped walnuts (optional)

Instructions

Mix all together. Do not overbeat. Bake for 1 hour at 350 F. Check after 35 minutes. Do not overbake.

Use a greased angelfood cake pan or a 9 x 13 pan.

item.title: Cherry Walnut Fruitcake
item.description:
This fruitcake is an easy- to-make classic holiday offering. If you like, make it ahead, wrap and freeze for entertaining and gift-giving.

Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2 eggs, lightly beaten
  • 1 jar (750 ml) mincemeat
  • 1 tin (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk
  • 2 cups chopped mixed glace fruit
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts
  • Whole glace cherries

Instructions

Grease two 9 x 5 loaf pans – set aside.

item.title: Cherry Cheese Cake
item.description:
• 1/2 to 1 cup icing sugar
  • 250 gr. Pkg cream cheese
  • 1 envelope Dream Whip or 1 cup whipping cream
  • 1 tin cherry pie filling
  • 1 graham crumb crust

Bake crumb crust at 350 degrees for 10 minutes. Cream cheese and sugar till nice and soft. Make Dream Whip and combine with cheese. Cover graham crust. Put cherries on top. Leave in refrigerator for 24 hours.

CRUST;

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup melted butter
  • 4 tbsp white sugar
  • 1-1/4 cup graham crumbs

Instructions

Combine and press in 9”x 9” pan.

item.title: Carrot Cake Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 medium-sized carrots, peeled & grated
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ cup raisins
  • ¾ tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup healthy margarine
  • ¼ tsp each ground ginger, nutmeg, allspice
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup dark brown sugar
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 1 egg
  • 2-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ cup buttermilk
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

  1.  Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Coat 12 muffin cups with a little margarine. Measure 1 cup grated carrot into a bowl. Add raisins. Melt ½ cup margarine in microwave or saucepan. Let cool to room temperature.

  2.  Place flours, then baking powder, soda, salt and spices into large bowl. Add sugar and stir with a fork until evenly blended. Make a well in center. Place egg, buttermilk and vanilla in another bowl. Whisk with a fork. Whisk in cooled margarine. Pour into well. Stir just until dry ingredients are moistened. Add carrots and raisins. Stir until evenly distributed.

  3.  Immediately spoon into muffin cups. Do not press down.

  4.  Bake in center of oven until a skewer inserted in center of muffin comes out almost clean, 17 to 20 minutes. Cool on a rack. Makes 12 muffins.

1 muffin – 235 Calories, 4 gr Proteins, 9 gr Fat, 36 gr Carbohydrate

item.title: Cheese and Onion Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 Tbsp white sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup shredded old cheddar cheese
  • 1 pkg onion soup mix
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • ¼ cup oil

Instructions

Stir together dry ingredients and stir in the cheese

Combine soup mix and milk. Let stand 5 min.

Beat egg, milk mixture and oil and add to dry ingredients mixing lightly just to combine.

item.title: Bran Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup shortening
  • 1/2 cup firmly packed brown sugar
  • 1/4 cup molasses
  • 2 eggs, unbeaten
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 1/2 tsp baking soda
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • 1-1/2 cup Quaker Natural Wheat Bran (Green Box)
  • 1/2 cup raisins

Instructions

Cream shortening and sugar together. Add molasses and eggs and beat together well. Add milk and then bran. Combine flour, baking power, soda and salt together. Add to liquid ingredients. Add raisins if desired. Place in greased muffin pans. Bake in 400 degree oven for l8 – 20 minutes. Makes l dozen large muffins.

item.title: Bisquick Strawberry Shortcake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2-1/3 cups Bisquick
  • 3 Tbsp melted butter
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1 quart strawberries, sliced and sweetened
  • 3 Tbsp sugar

Instructions

Heat oven to 425 F. Stir all ingredients except strawberries until soft fough forms.

Drop by 6 spoonfuls onto ungreased baking sheet.

Bake 10 – 12 minutes. Split; fill and top with strawberries and whipped cream, if desired.

item.title: Banana Muffins
item.description:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Blend or sift together:

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • Chopped walnuts (optional)

Make a well in center of the dry ingredients.

Combine and add:

Ingredients

  • 1 beaten egg
  • 2/3 cup milk
  • 1 cup well-mashed bananas
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil

Instructions

Stir only until combined. (The batter should be lumpy.) Fill greased muffin cups 2/3 full.

Bake in preheated oven for approx. 20 minutes.

item.title: All-Bran Muffins - No. 2
item.description:
Combine together:

  • 2 beaten eggs
  • 2 cups buttermilk
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 cups All-Bran (coarse)
  • 1 cup Mazola Oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Sift together, then add to the above:

Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup raisins
  • 2 tsp baking powder

Instructions

Fill medium-sized paper cups ¾ full. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes in a 375 degree oven until golden brown.

BB - I have no idea why this recipe has “No. 2” in its name. Likely the 2nd iteration of this, or just the 2nd Bran Muffin recipe she had. Regardless, I thought it was funny and topical with the type of recipe, so I left it in.

item.title: Royal Icing
item.description:
• 3 level Tbsp meringue powder
  • 500 gr (or 1.10 lb) sifted icing sugar
  • 3-1/2 oz warm water
  • ½ tsp cream of tartar

Combine ingredients, mixing slowly. Then beat at medium speed for 7 – 10 minutes. Utensils have to be grease-free. Icing dries rapidly so keep it covered with a damp cloth. To restore texture, re-beat slowly. Makes 3-1/2 cups.

OR:

Ingredients

  • 3 egg whites
  • 500 gr. (1.10 lb) sifted icing sugar
  • 1 tsp almond flavoring

Instructions

Whip at low speed till desired working consistency is reached. Keep covered with damp cloth while working flowers. Store tightly covered in fridge. Whip again before using.

item.title: Simple Caramel Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup lightly salted butter
  • 1-1/2 cups dark brown sugar
  • 1/3 cup milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 3 cups icing sugar

Instructions

  1.  Melt butter in small pan and stir in brown sugar.

  2.  Stir in milk and bring mixture just to a boil.

  3.  Cool to lukewarm. Stir in vanilla and icing sugar and beat until creamy.

Credit to Country Living for this recipe!

item.title: Rich Chocolate Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 squares unsweetened chocolate
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Melt chocolate in top of double boiler. Stir in icing sugar, beaten egg, butter and vanilla. Remove from heat at once and stir. Cool a little before spreading on brownies.

item.title: Rich Butter Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup butter
  • 2-1/2 or 2-3/4 cup sifted icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Cream butter well; stir in egg. Add sugar, a little at a time, beating until light and fluffy. Stir in vanilla.

Yield: enough for 2 pans.

item.title: Quick and Easy Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 envelope whipped topping mix (2 to 2-1/2 cup yield)
  • 1 pkg instant pudding mix (4 serving size)
  • 1-1/2 cup cold milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla, if desired

Instructions

In a chilled bowl, blend and whip topping mix, instant pudding, cold milk and vanilla until stiff (3-8 min.). Immediately frost cake and store in refrigerator. Serve chilled. Frosted cake may be frozen for storage

item.title: Miracle Icing
item.description:
WHITE SAUCE;

  • 1 cup milk
  • 4 tbsp flour

Cook as for white sauce, and let cool.

BEAT WELL;

Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla.

Instructions

When sauce is cooled, add the butter and sugar mixture, and BEAT WELL.

Makes enough for a layer cake.

item.title: Marshmallow Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1/3 cup water
  • Pinch of cream of tartar
  • Pinch salt
  • 2 egg whites
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Combine sugar, water, cream of tartar and salt in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, stir to dissolve sugar and continue to cook, without stirring, until mixture reaches soft ball stage of 240 F./115C.

Place egg whites in a bowl and beat with an electric mixer until white and frothy. Continue to beat, adding sugar mixture very slowly, until very light and fluffy. Beat in vanilla.

item.title: Fluffy Chocolate Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups icing sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 6 tablespoons butter
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • 4 squares bitter chocolate
  • ½ cup milk
  • 2 eggs

Instructions

Blend sugar, milk, eggs, salt and vanilla in a bowl over ice water. Melt chocolate and butter together. Add while warm to first mixture and beat until desired consistency.

item.title: Jell-O Seven Minute Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 egg whites, unbeaten
  • 1-1/4 cups sugar
  • 1 pkg Jell-O (any flavour)
  • Dash of salt
  • 1/3 cup water
  • 2 tsp light corn syrup

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in top of double boiler. Beat 1 minute to mix thoroughly. Place over boiling water and beat constantly with rotary beater (or at high speed of electric mixer) 7 minutes, or until frosting will stand in stiff peaks. (Stir frosting up from bottom and sides of pan occasionally.) Remove from boiling water. Beat 1 minute, or until thick enough to spread.

item.title: Dot's Flour Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 Tbsp flour
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup butter
  • ½ cup shortening
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp lemon extract or vanilla

Instructions

Mix flour, milk and egg and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens.

Cool. Combine sugar, butter and shortening, and then blend in cooled mixture. Beat until smooth and fluffy; mix in flavoring and spread on cake.

item.title: Creamy Strawberry Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup butter, shortening or margarine
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp lemon juice
  • 1 tsp (1/2 of a small packet) Strawberry unsweetened instant soft drink mix
  • 3-1/2 cups sifted icing sugar
  • ¼ cup milk

Instructions

Combine Crisco, salt, lemon juice, dry strawberry instant drink mix and 1 cup sugar.

Add milk and remaining sugar alternately and mix until smooth. Add milk or sugar as necessary for good spreading consistency.

item.title: Cream Cheese Frosting
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 4-oz pkg cream cheese
  • 2 cups icing sugar
  • 2 tbsp milk
  • ¼ tsp salt

Instructions

In beater bowl beat cheese until creamy then add sugar and salt and beat until smooth, adding milk slowly until of smooth almost runny spreading consistency.

item.title: Buttercream Icing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup Crisco (or ½ cup Crisco + ½ cup white margarine)
  • 1 kg (2.20 lbs) icing sugar
  • ½ cup water (I use a wee bit more – in icing I use to ice the cake – it’s easier to spread)
  • 1 tsp almond flavoring (I use 2 – 3 tsp of almond or coconut flavor)

Instructions

This icing is the easiest recipe for doing cakes. It has a creamy texture and is great to practice making flowers. And for the borders – just add a few drops of water.

item.title: Wild Rice Many-Grain Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup wild rice
  • 2 Tbsp granulated sugar
  • ½ cup warm water
  • 1 pkg dry yeast (or 1 Tbsp )
  • 5 cups (approx.) all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup fancy molasses
  • 2 Tbsp shortening
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ cup natural bran
  • ½ cup raw sunflower seeds
  • ¾ cup 7-grain cereal
  • ¾ cup rolled oats

Instructions

  1.  In saucepan, cook wild rice in 2 cups water (500 ml) water for about 45 minutes or until tender; drain and let cool.

  2.  Meanwhile, dissolve 1 tsp (5 ml) of the sugar in warm water. Sprinkle in yeast; let soften, stirring together until dissolved. Stir in ¼ cup (50 ml) of the flour to make a thin paste; set in warm place for 10 minutes or until bubbly.

  3.  In large bowl and using electric mixer, combine molasses, remaining sugar, shortening, salt and 2 cups (500 ml) warm water. In order, beat in bran, sunflower seeds, yeast mixture, wild rice, 7-grain cereal and rolled oats. With wooden spoon, beat in as much of the remaining flour as necessary to make firm dough. Turn out onto lightly floured surface; knead for about 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic, kneading in as much of the remaining flour as necessary..

  4.  Place in large greased bowl, turning to grease all over. Cover with plastic wrap; let rice in warm draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours.
